races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Lars Soldier
runners.spotlight: All three runs have been at the Curragh, big improvement second occasion to beat two subsequent winners in 5f maiden on testing ground; upped to 6f in Group 3 Marble Hill on Sunday, not doing himself justice on quicker ground after racing keenly when beaten over 6l; persisted with at this trip, needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Too keen in Marble Hill on Sunday; tries 6f again but needs to settle better

runners.name: Joga Bonito
runners.spotlight: Made 85,000gns as a yearling; closely related to Group-placed 1m/1m1f winner Silver Sword (RPR 106), half-brother to winners Ae Fond Kiss (7.3f; 67) and Mister Slovakia (Polish 9.5f); dam 2m AW winner (90); joint favourite, showed plenty of speed but green (hung left) and weakened inside final furlong on recent Roscommon debut over 7.5f; drop back in trip should suit.
runners.comment: Plenty of speed but didn't stay 7.5f on debut; drop back in trip should suit

runners.name: High King
runners.spotlight: Calyx colt; price rose from 55,000euros as a foal to 150,000gns as a yearling; closely related to 7f 2yo winner Look Around (inc Listed; RPR 105), half-brother to four winners inc Sara Lucille (French Group-placed 5f inc 2yo; 100); dam 2yo Group 3 winner; 10/1, green when never sighted on debut in Curragh maiden early this month; sure to come on from that.
runners.comment: Never sighted on debut in Curragh maiden early this month; sure to come on from that
runners.owner: Barry Fowler & John C Oxley & Justin Edward Casse

runners.name: Tatianna
runners.spotlight: Kameko filly; 20,000euros foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Qaroun (useful 7f/1m) and Puerto De Vega (6f/7f); encouraging debut in similar 5f race at Cork nine days ago but plenty more likely needed here.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut in similar 5f race at Cork nine days ago but plenty more likely needed

runners.name: Citrus Springs
runners.spotlight: Olympiad filly cost 65,000USD as a yearling and 300,000USD at the breeze-ups; half-sister to US winners Spirit Maker (1m-1m1f turf inc 2yo) and Not Guilty (5f AW); dam once-raced half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 2 winner Protonico, is granddaughter of US 8.5f Grade 1 winner Wild Spirit; very interesting debutante, likely has ground to suit (holds Group entry next month).
runners.comment: US-bred a 300,000USD breeze-up buy; very interesting debutante, likely has ground to suit

runners.name: Magical Moonlight
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army filly cost 12,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to Italian 6f winner Nalu; dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (RPR 96), closely related to 5.5f 2yo winner Miss Rosina; yard having a good season so respected but but juveniles tend to benefit from a run.
runners.comment: Yard having a good season so respected but but juveniles tend to benefit from a run

runners.name: Pisiffik Ginger
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly cost 22,000euros as a foal; first foal; dam 1m winner (RPR 90), half-sister to 1m3f/12.4f winner Sportingsilvermine; market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory filly cost 22,000euros as a foal; market best guide on debut

runners.name: I Ready
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army gelding; 10,000euros yearling; second foal; dam runner-up 6f 2yo (RPR 74), half-sister to winners Insignia Of Rank (1m Listed) and Mayleaf Shine (useful 5f inc 2yo); slow to break and green, not disgraced in the circumstances on Curragh debut last Saturday; quick return and should have learnt a lot.
runners.comment: Slow to break and green, not disgraced in the circumstances on Curragh debut last Saturday

runners.name: Granite Mountain
runners.spotlight: Last of 7 on Curragh debut on opening day of the season in testing conditions; best watched.
runners.comment: Last of 7 on Curragh debut on opening day of the season in testing conditions

races.race_name: Royal County Optional Claiming Race

runners.name: Gloriously Glam
runners.spotlight: Was gaining a fourth career win when landing similar 7f contest at Gowran eight days ago, beating Profit Surge a shade cosily; similar terms with that rival here but shorter trip a slight concern.
runners.comment: Recent Gowran claiming win over Profit Surge at 7f; drop in trip a slight concern
runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand

runners.name: Mehmar
runners.spotlight: Won twice over this trip for Michael O'Callaghan on turf in 2024, showing a liking for quick ground; placed in AW claimer over the winter but generally regressive; visor replaces cheekpieces here and looks to have something to find.
runners.comment: Regressive 6yo; visor replaces cheekpieces and looks to have something to find.

runners.name: Profit Surge
runners.spotlight: Maiden was placed three times in AW handicaps last winter and ran well above her handicap mark when pushing Gloriously Glam close in 7f Gowran claimer eight days ago, albeit when allowed to dictate a steady pace; handicap mark has gone up 9lb but meets that rival on same terms here; blinkers now tried and drops in trip.
runners.comment: Second to Gloriously Glam in 7f claimer 8 days ago; same terms and now blinkered

runners.name: Lismacbryan
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over this trip in Curragh handicap in 2024; good second in first-time cheekpieces at Naas last summer but little since; would be much better off with all of these in a handicap.
runners.comment: Would be much better off with all of these in a handicap

runners.name: Tawaazon
runners.spotlight: Durable sort; gained the most recent of his nine wins at Naas a year ago before good third at the Curragh; absent since July and looks to have a bit to find with some of these.
runners.comment: 9-time winner absent since July and looks to have a bit to find with some of these

runners.name: Spirit Of Eagles
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 5f at Down Royal three years ago; best run for some time when placed in Sligo handicap early this month off basement mark of 40; plenty on at the weights against most of her rivals here.
runners.comment: Recent Sligo run her best for some time but lots on at the weights here

runners.name: Three Sixteen
runners.spotlight: Given a mark of 67 after three maiden runs in second half of last season over different trips; hooded on return for new yard.
runners.comment: Three ordinary maiden runs last season; hooded on return for new yard

races.race_name: Fiver Fridays At Fairyhouse June 5th And 12th Handicap

runners.name: Gonna Be Golden
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; Dundalk maiden win in December (7f); tailed off on seasonal return and handicap debut at the Curragh last month (1m, soft); could improve plenty for quicker ground and drop to 6f and yard in form.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner, bombed out on soft on h'cap debut (1m), could improve for better ground

runners.name: Absoluteassignment
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; huge odds and no show in three 7f maidens on easy ground; best watched in first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: Modest form in 7f maidens, best watched on handicap debut in new tongue tie

runners.name: Stone Bear
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come on the AW, including over 6f on penultimate; ran on well for a 1l third of 16 off this mark at Naas on latest (5f, good); step back up to 6f a big positive; this a tougher race but looks progressive and leading claims.
runners.comment: Two-time AW winner, stayed on well over 5f at Naas latest, return to 6f a plus, big player

runners.name: Treasured Royal
runners.spotlight: Navan maiden winner last season; highly tried to no avail afterwards; poor seasonal return at Naas but back on song in second handicap start in first-time hood (retained) at that venue on latest (6f, good); should give another good account off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, back on song at Naas latest, in the mix again off 1lb higher

runners.name: Baiana
runners.spotlight: Promising effort on second start at Navan (6f, soft) last October; returned to win a Sligo fillies' maiden (6f, yld-sft) 25 days ago; could go well on handicap debut but good ground is a query.
runners.comment: Off the mark in Sligo fillies maiden latest, go well on h'cap debut if handles good ground

runners.name: Girl Bear
runners.spotlight: Close third on debut at Galway last August (7f, soft); promising effort on handicap debut under positive ride at Roscommon last month (7.5f, good); eased 1lb and this shorter trip could suit; in the mix.
runners.comment: Good run under positive ride at Roscommon, this shorter trip could suit, in the mix

runners.name: Jackie Jump Up
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante yet to encounter good ground; 2l to find with Baiana on Sligo running latest; hood (ran well in one before) and tongue tie added; in the mix if handles quicker conditions.
runners.comment: 2l behind Baiana latest, new headgear combination tried, in the mix if handles ground

runners.name: Reimagined
runners.spotlight: Won a Windsor maiden (6f, good) for Eve Johnson-Houghton; never involved on debut for this yard at the Curragh (6f, sft-hvy); 8lb lower now and these conditions more suitable; new headgear combination goes on.
runners.comment: No show on testing ground on stable bow latest, new headgear on, this ground more suitable

runners.name: Jampa Ling
runners.spotlight: Well-backed when off the mark at third attempt in 6f Dundalk maiden in February; has come up short in two handicaps at 5f-6f back there since; handicapper giving him a chance and blinkers go on but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner, beaten in AW handicaps twice since, blinkers on, others appeal more

runners.name: All Hail
runners.spotlight: Second runner for stable; no impact in first two maidens but much more like it when a 2l second at Navan (6f, good) when last seen last July; gelded since and returns for handicap debut with yard amongst winners; check market.
runners.comment: H'cap debutant, good run at Navan when last seen, gelded since, worth market check
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

races.race_name: 5 Euro General Admission Next Friday Handicap

runners.name: I'm Spartacus
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner remains a maiden on turf (0-22); has placed over C&D (good) off 3lb higher and shaped better than the bare result at the Curragh latest so not dismissed from an each-way perspective.
runners.comment: Six-time AW winner, 0-22 on turf but has placed over C&D off 3lb higher, each-way shout

runners.name: Oxford Circus
runners.spotlight: Good effort in 6f maiden on debut at Dundalk (6f) in February but hasn't built on that; yet to show winning potential on turf; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on promising AW debut, no sign of winning potential on turf, others preferred

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been knocking on the door this season; narrow defeat at Sligo on penultimate and beaten 2l when third of 17 at Naas latest; visor worn at Sligo returns and just 1lb higher here but best form on soft ground.
runners.comment: Just 1lb higher than when runner up at Sligo on penultimate, may need softer ground

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been running okay this season; narrowly denied off 1lb higher at Cork on latest (5f, soft); handles quicker ground; should be in the mix again back at 6f off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied at Cork (5f) latest, dropped 1lb, return to 6f fine, in the mix again

runners.name: Livingston Range
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 2-34 on turf; not a bad effort on seasonal return at Navan (6f, yielding); close second in this last year off 4lb higher; could go well if gets in.
runners.comment: Decent run on return at Navan, close second in this last year, go well if gets in; reserve

runners.name: Jackandthefox
runners.spotlight: 0-14 on turf but one of better efforts came last June when a 3.5l second over C&D off this mark; off since a below par run at Dundalk last August; might need this run.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D off this mark last June, off since last August, might just need this run

runners.name: Rattletheonionbag
runners.spotlight: First reserve; long standing maiden who has shown her best form on the AW; beaten 6l on return to turf at Sligo latest; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Maiden, best form on AW, beaten 6l on return to turf latest, others preferred; reserve

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Won two for Owen Burrows in 2024 but no joy yet for this stable; poor form in four runs this term; new blinkers need to transform.
runners.comment: Yet to win since joining this yard, out of form this season, new blinkers need to spark

runners.name: Son Of Wind
runners.spotlight: 5f AW winner in Britain in August 2024; failed to fire in 2025 and hasn't looked a winner in waiting in three runs for this stable; dropped 6lb for heavy defeat at Gowran latest (1m, gd-yld); can only watch down in trip.
runners.comment: AW winner, heavy defeats all three starts for this yard, mark sliding but can only watch

runners.name: El Fontenaro
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-14 on turf; best efforts have come over 5f; beaten 0.5l off 13lb higher at Down Royal in May 2025 and comeback at Sligo (6f, yld-sft) was not devoid of promise (denied clear run); eased 2lb; not discounted if backing that up.
runners.comment: 0-14 on turf, comeback run not devoid of promise at Sligo, eased 2lb, not discounted

runners.name: Reponse Finale
runners.spotlight: 0-14 on turf; best handicap run came last September at Cork when a 1.25l third of ten over 6f (yielding); 6lb lower now and had excuses for poor seasonal comeback latest but might prefer easier ground.
runners.comment: 0-14 on turf, 6lb lower than when a 1.25l third at Cork last Sept', may need easier ground

runners.name: Harry Longabaugh
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens and beat just one rival home on handicap debut at Naas on seasonal return; may have needed that and dropped 3lb but can only watch for now.
runners.comment: No improvement on handicap debut, dropped 3lb, can only be watched for now

runners.name: Run Forrest Run
runners.spotlight: Knows how to win (6-44 on turf) but losing run stretches back to October 2024; in poor form on AW when last seen in December; best watched on debut for new yard after break.
runners.comment: No win since October 2024, off since December, watch on debut for new yard

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 0-6 and has yet to show she is capable of winning a race; beaten 8l at Navan on latest; 5lb drop not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Yet to show winning potential, beaten 8l at Navan latest, others appeal more; reserve
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Rodeeve
runners.spotlight: Maiden whose best efforts have come over 7f on good and soft ground; weakened into a 4.75l fourth under positive ride at Gowran latest (7f, soft); well worth a go at this trip on that evidence and dropped 3lb; could go well.
runners.comment: Weakened late over 7f at Gowran latest, down 3lb, this strip could suit on that evidence

runners.name: Jazzy Dancer
runners.spotlight: No win on turf since 2022 but scored at Dundalk (7f) for the third time in February; poor on latest back at Dundalk (7f); 5lb lower mark on turf but has run poorly both previous starts over this C&D.
runners.comment: No turf win since 2022, AW win over 7f in Feb', previous runs over this C&D were poor

runners.name: Apache Star
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5.5f at Bath in 2023 but has come up dry since; no show in three starts for this stable but handicapper relenting and Colin Keane is 2-9 for this yard.
runners.comment: No win since 2023, modest first three runs for this yard, booking of Keane a positive

runners.name: Are You In Or Out
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Paul Stafford last season, including over C&D (gd-yld) off 6lb lower; lacks a run but won first time out last year; worth market check on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner, new yard, lacks a run but has gone well fresh, check the market

runners.name: Twotolose
runners.spotlight: Standout effort on second start in a Navan maiden (5f, yielding) but nowhere near that level since, including in first two handicaps; drops in trip after modest reappearance run at Leopardstown; others preferred.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on promise of Navan third, modest return at Leopardstown, drops in trip

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Likeable veteran whose last four wins have come over 7f; not a bad run at Gowran latest and on dangerous mark but probably vulnerable to younger legs at this trip.
runners.comment: Likeable veteran, last four wins at 7f, on dangerous mark but vulnerable at this trip
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

runners.name: Miqdaad
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner in 2024; one good run last year, a 0.5l second of 13 at Limerick in July; 2lb lower here but has been off since a below par run at Leopardstown last August; might need this.
runners.comment: Ran well at Limerick last year off 2lb lower, lacks a recent run, might need this

races.race_name: Book Your Hospitality At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap

runners.name: Elly Bay
runners.spotlight: Solid placed efforts in maidens last year for Johnny Murtagh; just okay efforts in both handicaps this term, latest when didn't seem to quite get home at Roscommon (6l off Fortuity).
runners.comment: Didn't seem to quite get home at Roscommon (6l off Fortuity) so has a fair bit to find

runners.name: Thirsty Owl
runners.spotlight: Definite signs of ability in three AW starts for Gavin Cromwell; good early speed before dropping away on yard debut in Roscommon handicap earlier this month (7.5f) in first-time cheekpieces (now left off); entitled to come on for that comeback run.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for recent comeback run; cheekpieces discarded

runners.name: One Cool Bear
runners.spotlight: Maiden with plenty of experience, notably twice runner-up in AW handicaps; plenty to find with Fortuity on recent Roscommon run though.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up in AW handicaps but plenty to find with Fortuity on recent Roscommon run

runners.name: Venetian Star
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in Down Royal nursery for Johnny Murtagh and has returned with two solid efforts for current yard without convincingly staying the mile trip, at the Curragh and when weakening late at Cork, both times on soft; drop back in trip and better ground makes him a major player.
runners.comment: Didn't quite get home over 1m both starts this term; big player dropped in trip

runners.name: Royal County Glory
runners.spotlight: Mostly campaigned on the AW, making the breakthrough over this trip last month off 57 under today's rider; decent ground should suit and respected off 5lb higher.
runners.comment: Last month's Dundalk win at this trip; 5lb higher but could have more to offer at 7f

runners.name: Caoilte
runners.spotlight: All five runs this year have been on the AW, notably fair fifth to Royal County Glory over this trip, most recently last of 10 over a mile last month; return to 7f may suit for turf debut but others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Return to 7f may suit for turf debut but others make greater appeal

runners.name: Fortuity
runners.spotlight: Much improved back on better ground at Roscommon recently, awkward start but plenty of speed when second to a clear-cut winner over 7.5f; mark unchanged and definite contender.
runners.comment: Much improved back on better ground at Roscommon recently; mark unchanged and a contender

runners.name: Kc Bear
runners.spotlight: Ended AW campaign winning 6f nursery in December, needing every yard after a slow start; returned to Dundalk in March when again not the best of starts, produced with every chance but just outstayed over a mile; drop back to 7f should suit and leading contender off what appears still a nice mark.
runners.comment: Just outstayed over 1m last time; drop back to 7f should suit and leading contender

runners.name: Merry Blacksmith
runners.spotlight: Too keen and didn't get home on recent handicap debut at Roscommon (7.5f); lightly raced and could still have more to offer, if settling.
runners.comment: Needs to settle better than at Roscommon; remains unexposed

runners.name: Chapel Island
runners.spotlight: Juvenile form with Michael Mulvany wasn't progressive, never competitive on handicap bow final start on soft; since joined Andy Oliver and handicapper has given him a chance.
runners.comment: Didn't progress at 2; new yard and handicapper has given him a chance

runners.name: Lady Kelly
runners.spotlight: Maiden form has been modest, all three runs on much softer ground; back in trip and blinkered for handicap debut on much quicker ground so plenty of reasons for improvement.
runners.comment: Back in trip and blinkered for handicap debut on quicker ground so reasons for improvement

runners.name: Millhone
runners.spotlight: Didn't lack market support but ran no sort of race after blowing the start on seasonal return in Gowran handicap early this month; caution advised.
runners.comment: Ran no sort of race after blowing the start on seasonal return in Gowran handicap
runners.owner: N J Hitchins & Mrs John M Weld

runners.name: Alandownunder
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in maidens last autumn on easy ground; plenty fresh on recent seasonal return on handicap debut at Roscommon and lots to find with a couple of these on that run.
runners.comment: Lots to find with a couple of these on recent Roscommon comeback run
runners.owner: Rob Egan/Kilmichael Racing Syndicate/Kieran P Cotter

races.race_name: Racing Again Next Friday Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Smithfield
runners.spotlight: Reappeared last month with best handicap effort (well-backed) yet in finishing fourth at Limerick over a mile, staying on; longer trip here could suit and one to consider.
runners.comment: Well-backed at Limerick when 1m looked inadequate; one to consider upped in trip now

runners.name: Proleek Prince
runners.spotlight: Useful form in 2024, winning over a mile at Punchestown and Dundalk; failed to prosper last year and lots to find on two efforts this term; tries this longer trip again.
runners.comment: Yet to hit form this year, tries this longer trip again

runners.name: Moyassr
runners.spotlight: Boasts a fine AW record (4 wins) but continues to struggle on turf (0-13); first turf attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: Much more effective AW performer; unproven over this far

runners.name: Perfect Judgement
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins, sole turf win came at Leopardstown (7f); late headway into midfield on recent yard debut at latter venue, remains unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Wins all at 7f/1m; remains unproven over this far

runners.name: Je Suis Celebre
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo touched off in 7f Kempton novice in January before bought out of Ian Williams' yard for 41,000gns; both maiden runs for new yard a bit below that level and now steps up in trip (bred to stay) for handicap debut with new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Steps up in trip for handicap debut with new headgear combination tried

runners.name: Out On Friday
runners.spotlight: Eight career wins include twice here at 1m4f; probably needed recent Roscommon comeback run, more expected of here with ground in his favour.
runners.comment: Probably needed Roscommon comeback run, more expected of here with ground in his favour

runners.name: Shanlieve
runners.spotlight: Some promise in 3yo maidens on good ground, softer conditions may not have suited on handicap debut in October; best watched on return.
runners.comment: Remains potential improver on good ground but best watched on seasonal return

runners.name: Casheldale Lass
runners.spotlight: Won Gowran and Naas handicaps last year at around this trip and back to form at Leopardstown earlier this month, staying on well when just touched off over this trip; 2lb rise okay, should again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Just denied at Leopardstown recently over this trip; 2lb rise okay, should be thereabouts

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Dundalk winner over this trip in March, her first success at the 15th attempt; faced tough task from out of the handicap at Musselburgh following month, back down in grade here.
runners.comment: Tough task last time; back in grade here so don't rule out

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Returned with a couple of good runs, notably staying today's trip well when only beaten a neck at Sligo; didn't get home in more testing conditions at Cork nine days ago (cheekpieces tried but now discarded) but remains of interest back on better ground.
runners.comment: Didn't get home in testing conditions at Cork 9 days ago but remains of interest

runners.name: Guy's Girl
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens last year at 1m-1m1f; not sure she stayed 1m4f on latest, back in trip here on second handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Not sure she stayed 1m4f on latest, back in trip here on second handicap attempt

runners.name: Pebble Island
runners.spotlight: Modest form for the Crisfords and nothing so far for current connections under both codes; down 10lb already this term.
runners.comment: Modest form for the Crisfords and nothing so far for current connections under both codes

runners.name: Doctor Grace
runners.spotlight: Recorded third turf win earlier this month at Roscommon under positive ride from today's rider (1m4f, good), overcoming wide draw and having been hampered; 5lb rise takes her back to a near career-high mark so interesting to see if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Overcame adversity for Roscommon win; 5lb rise so more needed

runners.name: Hosanna Power
runners.spotlight: No win since 1m2f Newbury success two years ago; form this spring poor; handicapper quick to drop him but no improvement; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Regressive this year and already down 19lbs

runners.name: Bright N Shine
runners.spotlight: Both wins came at this trip, most recently at Down Royal in 2024 off 64 under today's rider; absent since moderate second in 2m Tramore handicap hurdle a year ago so may need this.
runners.comment: Trip/ground ideal but year's absence a concern

runners.name: Anchors Away
runners.spotlight: Much-improved when third in Dundalk AW maiden in January, following month well held on handicap debut over same C&D; tongue-tie fitted for turf return, others preferred.
runners.comment: Tongue-tie fitted for turf return, others preferred

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Guardian Of Realm
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three starts in maidens at 7f-1m on the AW last year; hooded on recent Naas return but too keen and below best over a mile; upped in trip now with questions to answer and lots to find with Mai Dahlia on that run.
runners.comment: Too keen in first-time hood on recent turf debut; upped in trip now with a bit to prove

runners.name: Mai Dahlia
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot half-sister to winners Ameerat Jumaira (Group-placed 10.6f-1m4f inc AW; RPR 99) and Jujubella (1m2f-12.4f inc AW; 78); dam unraced half-sister to UAE 1m2f Group 1 winner North America;  promising Naas debut 11 days ago, coming home strongly from off the pace, having not had a clear run, when beaten a head and neck in fillies' maiden over 1m; longer trip set to suit and could be the one to beat.
runners.comment: Strong-finishing Naas debut third; longer trip set to suit and could be the one to beat

runners.name: Mathoura
runners.spotlight: Calyx filly a 30,000euros foal and 65,000euros yearling; half-sister to French winners Illois (9.5f/1m2f AW) and Imbleville (10.5f AW); dam 9.5f-10.7f winner (inc AW), out of half-sister to 1m2f BC Filly & Mare Turf winner Starine; good introduction in Gowran fillies' maiden early this month, plenty green when chasing home a potentially smart sort over 9.5f; definite player.
runners.comment: Recent Gowran debut second makes her a definite player

runners.name: Purely
runners.spotlight: Siyouni filly has looked fairly ordinary to-date, most recently when tried blinkered in good-class Leopardstown fillies' maiden last month; tries a new trip.
runners.comment: Siyouni filly has looked fairly ordinary to-date; tries a new trip
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Mandara Mist
runners.spotlight: Mehmas half-sister to winners Age Of Kings (6f 2yo/7f Group 3; RPR 113) and Dunamase (1m AW 2yo; 85); dam 1m-1m2f winner (inc 2yo Group 2; 110), half-sister to Australian 11.5f Group 3 winner Beyond Thankful; tongue-tied for debut, obvious respect from top yard.
runners.comment: Mehmas half-sister to Group 3 7f winner Age Of Kings; obvious respect from top yard.

runners.name: Monamie
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly out of a Galileo mare who was a 1m4f winner; never recovered from a slow start on recent Roscommon debut (maiden claimer); best watched in a much stronger race here.
runners.comment: Never recovered from a slow start on recent Roscommon debut; best watched

runners.name: Six Wings
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in maidens at Naas and Roscommon earlier this month; eligible for a mark after this.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in two maidens earlier this month; eligible for a mark after this

races.race_name: Visit attheraces.com EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Fern Clyde
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on her debut when just under 2l fourth of eight over C&D (good) 12 days ago; that form is below the level it usually requires to win a race like this so she'll almost certainly need to improve again.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut when fourth over C&D latest but it's likely more will be needed

runners.name: Aura Of Melania
runners.spotlight: Cost 12,000gns at the breeze-ups and is out of a mare who was runner-up from 1m-1m4f (including 2yo; RPR 78); the Roger Fell stable is going well but isn't a regular source of first-time-out 2yo success.
runners.comment: From an in-form yard but not one known for first-time-out 2yo success

runners.name: Etonnante
runners.spotlight: This second foal of a Listed-placed 1m AW winner is a half-sister to French Group-placed 1m AW winner Eponine (including 2yo; RPR 101); the outside stall isn't ideal for a newcomer but she makes obvious paper appeal for her very powerful yard.
runners.comment: Isn't ideally drawn but makes obvious paper appeal for her powerful yard

runners.name: Sovereign Beach
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns breeze-up buy who is a half-sister to four winners, notably So Majestic (Listed-placed 5f; RPR 97); stable has yet to hit the target with 2yos in 2026 but has enjoyed plenty of first-time-out juvenile success in recent years; would very much enter the reckoning if the betting vibes are strong.
runners.comment: 62,000gns breeze-up purchase from a yard no stranger to first-time-out success

runners.name: Bladey Lady
runners.spotlight: Minzaal half-sister to six winners but didn't achieve much when beaten just under 8l into third on her 5f Beverley debut (good to firm) 16 days ago; may do better but Fern Clyde is the more obvious of the Tim Easterby pair.
runners.comment: Only poor form when well-held third on Beverley debut

runners.name: Lady Branksome
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite for last week's 6f Kempton debut but was trapped wider than ideal from an outside draw and faded inside the final furlong; has the rail draw this time; likely improver.
runners.comment: Trapped wide and faded into fifth on last week's AW debut; likely improver; well drawn too

runners.name: Suzie May
runners.spotlight: Poor form in two outings at Beverley, finishing behind the reopposing Bladey Lady when a remote fourth latterly.
runners.comment: Poor form in two runs at Beverley, finishing behind reopposing Bladey Lady latterly

races.race_name: Book Now At ripon-races.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: Exposed, having raced 19 times, but has placed three times at up to 1m at Redcar (good/good to firm) this spring; handily drawn for one who tends to race close to the pace; should go well for in-form Roger Fell.
runners.comment: 1-19 but in good form this spring and has a handy inside draw here; player

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) in April but has failed to back that up twice since (including C&D).
runners.comment: Dead-heated at Wetherby in April but below par twice since, including C&D

runners.name: Captain Bruce
runners.spotlight: Won 7f/1m2f AW handicaps for Nigel Tinkler last year but has finished tailed off on all three starts this term, including back on turf for new yard 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Two AW wins last year but well beaten this term, including for new yard latest

runners.name: Contemplation
runners.spotlight: 0-4 for James Ferguson and hasn't threatened in three runs for new yard.
runners.comment: Hasn't threatened in three runs for new yard, taking record to 0-7

runners.name: Regal Knight
runners.spotlight: No significant impact in a pair of 1m2f handicaps this year, hanging under pressure at Beverley last time; a drop back in trip needs to help him find more.
runners.comment: Held in two 1m2f handicaps this year and return to 1m needs to spark improvement
runners.owner: D Bishop P Robinson S Bland T Coyle

runners.name: That's Enough
runners.spotlight: Raced on AW; 66-1 when well held on 7f Kempton handicap debut four weeks ago; still early days but a switch to turf needs to spark improvement.
runners.comment: Raced on AW, finishing well held on handicap debut latest; improvement needed
runners.owner: Commercium II And Whitsbury Manor Stud

runners.name: Eva The Deeva
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous in three outings over 6f/7f this spring but stands a much more realistic chance now handicapping and the Nigel Tinkler team has really hit form; improvement would come as no surprise.
runners.comment: Makes handicap debut with her yard in form; improvement wouldn't surprise

runners.name: Crystal Aurora
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; third on 6f Pontefract handicap debut (soft) last autumn but well held in two AW outings over the winter; revival needed after a break.
runners.comment: Third on turf handicap debut last autumn but well beaten on AW over winter; off 131 days

runners.name: Vega's Virtue
runners.spotlight: No significant impact in handicaps over 7f at Doncaster and 6f here this spring; still fairly early days but needs a combination of a step up in trip and first-time cheekpieces to make a difference.
runners.comment: Needs a step up in trip and first-time cheekpieces to spark improvement

runners.name: Wondrous
runners.spotlight: Beat only a couple home in three qualifying runs over 6f last year but rates a potential improver now handicapping over a longer trip on her return; market support would look significant.
runners.comment: Well held in three 6f runs last year but potential improvement in handicaps

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Suited by drop back in trip when scoring on this month's handicap debut at Hamilton (6f, good) and probably bettered that performance when close second over C&D (good) a fortnight later; back in action before 1lb nudge takes effect and nicely drawn towards nearside rail; big player.
runners.comment: Has gone close over C&D since this month's winning handicap debut at Hamilton; big player

runners.name: Great Success
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only four runs but was beaten about 19l after racing too freely on last month's seasonal/handicap debut (7f) and is drawn on the wing for this sprint return.
runners.comment: Still unexposed but was beaten 19l after racing too freely on seasonal/handicap debut (7f)

runners.name: North West Gal
runners.spotlight: Ended four-race 2yo season with 5f AW novice win in November and made encouraging reappearance when fifth in Catterick handicap (6f, good; short of room towards finish) this month; contender if that recent outing brought her on.
runners.comment: Made encouraging seasonal/handicap debut this month; player if coming on for the run

runners.name: Head For Freedom
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging seasonal debut when fourth of 13 over C&D (good to soft) last month and had some excuses when below that form over 5f at Beverley 24 days ago (suffered interference in first half of race); on a good mark now and enters calculations.
runners.comment: Encouraging fourth over C&D on seasonal debut and had some excuses last time

runners.name: Black Missile
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on all three 2yo starts (5f-6f) and again showed clear promise when fifth of 14 on recent seasonal/handicap debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft), despite racing too freely in the early stages and hanging left at the business end; back up in trip and 2lb lower here; a possible.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth of 14 on seasonal/handicap debut, despite doing a few things wrong

runners.name: Northern Tempest
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten after racing too freely in two AW handicaps (6f/7f) this spring and has now been unplaced all five starts; stable has a much more obvious chance with Simba's Pride.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten after racing too freely in two AW handicaps this spring; now 0-5 overall
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods

runners.name: Valor Spirit
runners.spotlight: 0-8 as a 2yo for Richard Fahey, albeit having made the frame six times; changed hands for 20,000gns in the autumn and was subsequently gelding; reappears on a workable mark; market may guide.
runners.comment: 0-8 as a 2yo for Richard Fahey; makes seasonal/stable debut having been gelded

runners.name: Uncertainty
runners.spotlight: Second in 7f AW nursery on final run for Richard Hannon but well beaten on both starts for new stable, latterly in 6f Doncaster handicap on seasonal reappearance 12 days ago; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts for current stable; others have much less to prove

runners.name: Come On Over
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was safely held over 5f on first two starts for new stable this spring, albeit having encountered traffic on first occasion; ought to be suited by this move back up in trip, though, and may yet come good for David O'Meara.
runners.comment: Started off for new yard with two low-key 5f runs this spring; this return to 6f will suit
runners.owner: Come On Over Syndicate & A H Yates Ltd

runners.name: Solar Cooper
runners.spotlight: Started off for new stable with two down-the-field runs last month but bounced back to form with clear C&D (good) win in first-time cheekpices 11 days ago; not ideally drawn today and goes without cheekpieces this time but still respected under 6lb penalty (due to go up that amount anyway).
runners.comment: Bounced back to form with C&D win in new cheekpices (missing today) last week; 6lb penalty

runners.name: Country Artiste
runners.spotlight: Started off for Craig Lidster with two very underwhelming AW runs in January (6f/5f) and is now 0-9 overall; difficult to enthuse over on first start for four months.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on both AW starts for new stable in January; returns from four-month break

races.race_name: Ripon Cathedral Handicap

runners.name: Financer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three times last season, including C&D; respectable fourth of ten to Spioradalta on C&D reappearance (good to soft); well beaten at the York Dante meeting since but should find this a bit easier.
runners.comment: Has some decent C&D form and should find this easier than the York Dante meeting last time
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: Front-running 11yo with seven course wins, the latest gained over 1m (good) last spring; creditable placed efforts on his last three starts (including C&D) and should give it another good shot, for all this is a higher grade than he's been contesting.
runners.comment: Seven wins here; recent placed efforts show there's still plenty of life in his 11yo legs

runners.name: Parole d'Oro
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Epsom (7f, good to firm) as a 2yo, though he twice finished runner-up last year, including at York (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) in October; didn't enjoy the smoothest run and far from disgraced in good Newbury handicap on reappearance; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Good second at York on final 3yo start; better than result on reappearance; could go well

runners.name: Prince Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Narrow win at Goodwood (1m4f, good to firm) for Ralph Beckett last summer; not disgraced on 1m4f course reappearance for new yard but went backwards from that at the York Dante meeting since; the first-time cheekpieces he wore there are quickly discarded; also down in trip.
runners.comment: Went backwards from 1m4f course reappearance fifth when well held at York; down in trip

runners.name: Austrian Theory
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and went backwards from C&D reappearance fifth to Spioradalta when well held at Hamilton a fortnight ago; on a career-low mark but need to see more.
runners.comment: Went backwards from C&D reappearance when well held at Hamilton 13 days ago

runners.name: Londoner
runners.spotlight: Usually thereabouts, including in AW campaign this year which culminated in a Lingfield 1m2f win in April; respectable fifth of nine off this mark at Chester (extended 1m2f, good) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: AW win in April; fair fifth off this mark at Chester since

runners.name: Spirit Of Acklam
runners.spotlight: Scored with a fair degree of authority at Ayr (1m2f, good; from 2lb lower) in April 2025; absent since toiling at Chester 362 days ago, which raises concerns, but he did win over C&D on his 2024 reappearance; the betting should help to guide expectations.
runners.comment: Off for a year but C&D win on 2024 reappearance shows he's capable when fresh

runners.name: Spioradalta
runners.spotlight: Enhanced his good Ripon record with a narrow win over this trip on good to soft last month; faded to finish well held at Chester since (Londoner ahead of him) but a revival can't be ruled out back here.
runners.comment: Landed C&D win number three in April; held at Chester since but can't rule out back here

runners.name: El Matador
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has been a good second at Lingfield (7f, AW) and Redcar (1m, good) this spring; hasn't necessarily shaped as if crying out for this trip but he's likely to be bang there again if his stamina holds.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 7f (AW) and 1m (turf) this spring; player if his stamina holds

runners.name: James McHenry
runners.spotlight: Course winner who was runner-up in the 2024 Cambridgeshire but has been lightly raced since; his keeping-on fifth of 12 over 1m1f at Musselburgh in April suggests most of that ability remains but he failed to build on it tackling 1m2f for the first time when fourth at Redcar (good to firm) four weeks ago; first-time cheekpieces worn at Redcar are retained.
runners.comment: Former course winner; still useful but latest 1m2f Redcar fourth no more than respectable

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 519 Handicap

runners.name: Jet Warrior
runners.spotlight: Came good with an easy 6f win at Ayr (good to firm) under Clifford Lee last week; 5lb well in under a penalty compared to his future mark so his claims are obvious.
runners.comment: Came good with easy Ayr win last week and well treated under a penalty

runners.name: Tickets
runners.spotlight: Short of room before catching the eye with late headway for fifth over 7f at Southwell three weeks ago; has done most of his winning on AW but a fast-ground win at Redcar last spring shows he's effective on turf; Jet Warrior has to be considered the stable number one but he's still of interest.
runners.comment: Jet Warrior looks the stable number one but this one did catch the eye on AW last time

runners.name: Hurstwood
runners.spotlight: Won this race in 2023 and 2025; sharper for his reappearance when respectable fourth of 12 at Redcar (6f, good) three weeks ago and may come on again; a must for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Fair fourth at Redcar latest; a must for the shortlist having won this in 2023 and 2025
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Ridgemaster
runners.spotlight: Caused a 66-1 shock when winning a 7f handicap at Newcastle on his stable debut in December but three heavy defeats have followed, including dropped to 6f back on turf last time.
runners.comment: Sprung 66-1 surprise on AW yard debut in December but three heavy defeats have followed

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: Arrives out of sorts (well beaten at Nottingham just nine days ago) but a revival can't be completely ruled out given he won twice over C&D (good) last May.
runners.comment: Arrives out of sorts but he did win twice over C&D last May

runners.name: Night Emperor
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Hugo Palmer; fared a lot better than on his AW stable debut in February when sixth (Hurstwood fourth) of 12 at Redcar (6f, good) three weeks ago but will need to step forward again.
runners.comment: Better signs for new yard when sixth of 12 at Redcar last time but more needed again

runners.name: Tarlac
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 19 starts came over C&D last May but he's falling in the weights without giving any obvious indication he's likely to take advantage; a first-time visor replaces the blinkers worn last time.
runners.comment: Sole win came over C&D but not obviously in good enough form to capitalise on falling mark
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Solar Biricz
runners.spotlight: Better signs for new stable when fourth at Redcar (5f, good to firm) in a first-time visor in April but the headgear didn't work anywhere near as well at Thirsk 12 days ago and is left off now.
runners.comment: Well held either side of respectable fourth at Redcar last month; others are more obvious

runners.name: Angel Of England
runners.spotlight: Went desperately close at Newcastle (6f) in December but has struggled for form since.
runners.comment: Went close on AW in December but has struggled for form since
runners.owner: The Jocks And The Geordies II

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap

runners.name: Something
runners.spotlight: Made all in this race off 2lb higher on good to soft for Jedd O'Keeffe last year; drew a blank in six outings for Stuart Williams but latest 1m4f Doncaster third on good to firm was respectable; sold for 8,000gns since; could make another bold bid in this race.
runners.comment: Won this race last year; respectable third last month; should have a say for new yard

runners.name: Sir Edward Lear
runners.spotlight: This course scorer might have needed his recent reappearance and is well treated if bouncing back to his best, having returned to the mark he defied at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) last August; also went close over C&D last summer; cheekpieces off, visor back on; yard hasn't been as prolific as usual in the last couple of weeks but that's likely to change before long.
runners.comment: Might have needed recent return; back on winning mark so good claims if returning to best

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: Went close more than once for Harry Charlton but left that yard an eight-race maiden; hasn't threatened to put that right in four runs for new stable but went with a bit more promise when sixth of 11 over 1m3f at Hamilton 11 days ago, keeping on from off the pace after missing the break again; due to be 2lb lower from the weekend.
runners.comment: Not disgraced at Hamilton latest but never threatened and now 0-12; can start slowly

runners.name: Spartan Times
runners.spotlight: Notched four Flat wins last year, including over 1m3f here on good; returned from a break to win over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m, good) this month; only a fair fourth at Aintree since but still respected back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Four Flat wins in 2025, including here; won over hurdles this month and fair fourth since

runners.name: So Alex
runners.spotlight: Progressed in his first three handicaps, winning over 1m4f on Wolverhampton's Tapeta in November/December; something can't have been right when well held there just before Christmas; absent subsequently and makes first turf start since his debut; remains with potential.
runners.comment: Well held on AW before Christmas but progressed well prior to that and retains potential

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: Made it two wins in last three starts when seeing off seven rivals at Musselburgh (5f, good) last Thursday; a 5lb penalty shouldn't prevent another bold bid in her current mood.
runners.comment: Has won two of last three and a 5lb penalty shouldn't prevent another bold bid

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: Doesn't always look the easiest of rides but recent efforts respectable (fifth of 17 at Thirsk latest) and he's been successful on the last two occasions he's dropped into a 0-70; considered.
runners.comment: Recent efforts respectable and has a good 0-70 record; enters calculations
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Dc Cogent
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner; 0-14 in handicaps but is in a consistent vein of form, reaching the frame three times this spring, including third to Herakles at Nottingham (5f, good); met trouble when a beaten favourite at Carlisle last week and can have that effort ignored.
runners.comment: 0-14 in handicaps but in decent form this spring and easy to ignore latest run (hampered)
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Wrestling Revenue
runners.spotlight: Well backed when winning a 5f handicap at Pontefract (soft) last October but hasn't matched that since, including beating only one home over C&D three weeks ago; back on the mark he defied at Pontefract but would need to see money for him again.
runners.comment: Below par of late but back on last winning mark and worth a look in the betting

runners.name: Marajito
runners.spotlight: Made all in maiden handicap at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last June; disappointing after that in 2025 but more encouraging signs when fifth of nine to Herakles (Dc Cogent third) on Nottingham reappearance 19 days ago, fading late as if the outing was just needed; enters calculations having dipped 2lb below her winning mark.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way behind two of these on Nottingham reappearance; on good mark

runners.name: Herakles
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; consistent in defeat on AW before making a successful return to turf at Nottingham (5f, good) 19 days ago, with reopposing Dc Cogent and Marajito behind in third and fifth; did that quite comfortably but a 6lb rise puts him 3lb above his highest win mark of 66; has also yet to score back-to-back in his 41-race career.
runners.comment: Winning return to turf at Nottingham this month but has yet to score back-to-back

runners.name: Our Absent Friends
runners.spotlight: Seven wins, the latest at Newcastle in December off a 6lb lower mark; good second off 1lb lower back at Newcastle in March but well held back on turf at Doncaster later that month; freshened up since.
runners.comment: Good second at Newcastle in March but well held at Doncaster later in month; off since

runners.name: South Parade
runners.spotlight: On a dangerous mark but she doesn't look in much form, finishing down the field again at Thirsk (Tees Aggregates several places ahead of her) 12 days ago; needs to rediscover her spark.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but she doesn't look in much form
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

races.race_name: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Pierian
runners.spotlight: Dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Irish Oaks); made a promising start when a close second at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) last month and she built on that when finishing well to win at Salisbury (1m2f, good) two weeks ago; looks a useful prospect and she has lots in hand on the figures over her sole rival.
runners.comment: Finished well when winning at Salisbury and she looks useful; should be hard to beat
runners.owner: Lord N P V J Rothschild

runners.name: Spirit Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Closely related to a 1m4f AW winner but he's struggled at biggish prices at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm) and Haydock (11.6f, soft) this month; still early days but this is a daunting task and handicaps will be more suitable.
runners.comment: Has struggled in two runs this month and handicaps will be more suitable

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Al Hudaiba
runners.spotlight: 5-1 and he stabilised a yard wobble when looking green but still coming out on top in six-runner Newmarket novice (6f, good) on his debut a fortnight ago; that form is yet to be tested but the runner-up topped an April breeze-up sale at £880,000 so it will be disappointing if it doesn't work out; the one to aim at under his penalty.
runners.comment: Won Newmarket novice (6f, good) on debut a fortnight ago; the one to beat under a penalty

runners.name: Eagle Island
runners.spotlight: By Camelot; January (ninth) foal; 150,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Martlet (1m2f-1m4f including Listed; RPR 110), Stowell (Group-placed 1m4f; 109), Keswick (9.4f/1m2f AW; 87) and Palmerston (1m/8.4f; 86); dam 1m3f winner (90), out of Irish Oaks winner; yard had its first 2yo winner of the year fairly recently; market check essential.
runners.comment: By Camelot; January foal; 150,000gns yearling; good family and market check essential

runners.name: Bincimbal
runners.spotlight: Late-March first foal by Too Darn Hot; 325,000gns yearling; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 99), closely related to 1m Group 3 winner Komorebi, half-sister to winners Bright Melody (Group-placed 1m1f/1m2f) and Hawk Power (Listed-placed 1m2f), out of Group 1 Fillies' Mile winner; first juvenile runner of the year for his stable and sales price/pedigree demand a market check.
runners.comment: Late-March foal by Too Darn Hot; 325,000gns yearling; sales price/pedigree are positives

runners.name: Byzantine
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica May (seventh) foal; 65,000euros yearling, 86,000euros breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to winners Open Secret (1m6f Flat, RPR 81; 2m3f hurdle) and Lone Fighter (7f AW; 72); dam 7.6f 2yo winner (98), half-sister to a useful 1m-1m6f Flat and 2m/2m5f hurdle winner; foaling date and mixed pedigree muddy the water but yard is off the mark with this year's 2yos and the betting should provide plenty of clues.
runners.comment: St Mark's Basilica May foal; 65,000euros yearling, 86,000euros breeze-up 2yo; check market

runners.name: Shiyam
runners.spotlight: March (fifth) foal; 200,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Luther (7f/1m including 2yo Listed/US Grade 2; RPR 115), Good Gracious (Listed-placed 7f AW 2yo/1m turf; 101) and Offer And Receive (1m 2yo/Swedish 7f; 79); dam 7.6f 2yo/1m2f Group 3 winner (101); many an eye will be trained on this first representative as a sire for brilliant racehorse Baaeed so fascinating to see how he goes in the market before showing what he can do.
runners.comment: March foal; 200,000gns yearling; fascinating first runner by the brilliant Baaeed

races.race_name: Norwich Inns Handicap

runners.name: Harlington
runners.spotlight: Six time winner who won three in a row in January/February and he ran another good race when third at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) last month; won on turf last summer but that was off a much lower mark and he needs to transfer his progress back to this sphere.
runners.comment: Three wins on AW this year but he needs to transfer his progress back to turf

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; won in good style on handicap debut at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) 17 days ago and he followed up with an odds-on win at Lingfield (11.6f, good) last week; only 4lb higher under a penalty here and he's open to more progress in middle-distance handicaps; big player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who is 2-2 in handicaps and he's open to more progress; big player
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: King's Hand
runners.spotlight: Polytrack winner for Sir Mark Prescott in January; two good runs to start off his time with Joseph Parr and he took a step forward when seeing off seven rivals at Lingfield (1m2f, good) ten days ago; this is tougher under a penalty but he did well to overcome a slow start last time and he's still unexposed on turf; respected.
runners.comment: Did well to score after a slow start at Lingfield ten days ago; respected under penalty

runners.name: Man Of Desert
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has form figures of 0487057 in handicaps; had some traffic issues when prominent in the market at Kempton last week but this is competitive back on turf and he still has stamina to prove at this trip; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who has form figures of 0487057 in handicaps; others preferred

runners.name: Give Me The Night
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has not lived up to his initial promise and he's been well held in five handicaps (7f/1m); now 10lb lower than for his opening mark but he needs a transformation at this new trip.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden and he needs a transformation at this new trip

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Another Abbot
runners.spotlight: Beaten just over 2l when an encouraging seventh of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) at the start of the month; both of last year's wins came at 6f on a sound surface so has conditions to suit and he's a candidate to step forward.
runners.comment: Return to 6f should suit so might build on encouraging 5f reappearance

runners.name: First Folio
runners.spotlight: Five visits here have resulted in three wins and two places, all at 6f; made an encouraging return when fourth of nine at Ripon three weeks ago and he's slipped to a dangerous mark; tempting.
runners.comment: Three wins and two places from five C&D starts make him impossible to ignore

runners.name: Carbine Harvester
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two good AW runs at Kempton; down the field in three AW starts this year and he had been given a wind op before weakening on his return to turf at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago; nothing wrong with this mark and he drops in grade but the jury's out at the moment.
runners.comment: Nothing wrong with this mark but the jury's out after some lesser efforts this year

runners.name: Neyva's Angel
runners.spotlight: Broke her duck in a Southwell handicap last month then bungled the start before seeing little daylight when fourth of 18 at Ascot (6f, good to firm; Fantasy Master third) 19 days ago; looked to finish that event with a fair bit more to give and she's a key player.
runners.comment: One place behind Fantasy Master last time; looked unlucky and could well gain her revenge

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Quickly clicked into gear for this yard with an eyeshield fitted, scoring twice on AW in April before close second at Lingfield (also 6f, good) last Monday; due for a 1lb nudge in future and he can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Quickly clicked into gear for this yard with eyeshield on; solid second latest; feared

runners.name: Izzy Fast
runners.spotlight: Made a proper splash when going in at 50-1 on her reappearance in C&D maiden (good; third start) in April but contrasting fortunes when beaten 18l on her handicap debut at Newmarket 12 days ago; that was too bad to be true but does lower expectations somewhat.
runners.comment: 50-1 win on her reappearance in C&D maiden but beaten 18l on handicap debut next time

runners.name: Style King
runners.spotlight: 0-5 on turf; didn't build on his second at Wolverhampton in April when favourite but flopping at Southwell (also 6f, AW) a fortnight later; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 0-5 on turf; flopped on AW last time and others hold more pressing claims

runners.name: Twilight Fun
runners.spotlight: Tends to lead; started the year well on AW but it all went wrong at Newcastle in March and he could only beat one home in seven-runner 5f course handicap a fortnight ago; easy ground probably no help that day and it's not impossible this C&D winner can stage a revival.
runners.comment: Last two runs not good but had excuses; not impossible this C&D winner can stage a revival

runners.name: Fantasy Master
runners.spotlight: Wasted little time getting off the mark for this yard when scoring at Chelmsford (AW) in December and it was a fine return when flying out of the stalls before finishing third of 18 at Ascot (again 6f, good to firm; Neyva's Angel fourth) 19 days ago; said to look "absolutely superb" in a recent stable tour and he's a must for the shortlist off the same mark.
runners.comment: Fine third on return; not sure to confirm places with Neyva's Angel but still a key player

runners.name: Valley Ofthe Kings
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins last year include one at 6f; didn't run at all badly when midfield in a stronger handicap than this at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) in March but he finished last of nine at Catterick the following month; connections blamed the undulations that day and he'll have no such concerns this time.
runners.comment: Connections blamed undulations for poor effort latest so this flatter track should help

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Typeface
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner but has mixed record since his emphatic win at Ffos Las (1m, good) last August; didn't live up to market expectations on Doncaster reappearance (good to soft) 12 days ago but he missed the break and may have needed that run; should be sharper this time and has possibilities if he can rediscover his spark.
runners.comment: Has mixed record since his last win but he's a player if he can rediscover his spark

runners.name: Charlatan
runners.spotlight: Gained his fifth AW success when winning at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December; mixed results in four subsequent runs but he rallied for fourth at Southwell (7f, AW) in March; now returns to turf but he has placed form in this sphere and is not ruled out back up in trip.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner; record of 0-7 on turf but he could be dangerous back in this sphere

runners.name: Merapi
runners.spotlight: 0-14 but she finished runner-up twice here (1m/1m1f) last month and had another close call at Wolverhampton two weeks ago; on same mark as for her last two runs and she should make another bold bid; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 0-14 but runner-up in last three starts and two of those were at Yarmouth; respected

runners.name: King Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on his 3yo form but 2025 was disappointing and he's struggled in both runs this year; mark continues to fall but he needs a needs a big turnaround in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but he needs a big turnaround in first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: El Fox
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but she's reached the frame in four of her handicaps including when fourth on C&D reappearance (good) two weeks ago; that was behind a major improver and she's a player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: 0-9 but she's reached the frame in four handicaps including over C&D; in the mix

runners.name: Siam Fox
runners.spotlight: C&D winner but his last success was almost two years ago; very well treated on old form but he's struggled in last five starts including after wind surgery here (1m good; 22-1) two weeks ago; blinkers are now tried and cheekpieces are removed.
runners.comment: His last win was almost two years ago and he's struggled in last five runs; new headgear

runners.name: Suitcase Smith
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo but he's not finished closer than 6l to a winner and was a remote seventh over C&D (good) two weeks ago; dropped another 3lb but he's hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he's not finished closer than 6l to a winner; hard to fancy

runners.name: Regal Guest
runners.spotlight: Promising third at Kempton (7f, AW) last March on his second start but has beaten only one rival in three handicaps since; still lightly raced but he needs a transformation at this new trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but he's beaten only one rival in three handicaps; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Kitty's Dream
runners.spotlight: Struggled in six runs for Alice Haynes including three handicaps (7f-8.6f); returns for new yard after 234 days off and she has a lot to prove; equipment removed.
runners.comment: Struggled in six runs for Alice Haynes and has a lot to prove on return for new yard

races.race_name: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Mares' Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Fox Wallace
runners.spotlight: Has lost her way badly since her win/place form over fences last summer; cheekpieces tried last time are swiftly removed; couldn't trust.
runners.comment: Has lost her way badly since her win/place form over fences last summer
runners.owner: M Enticott / Ella Pickard Racing Club

runners.name: Caterpillar Girl
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper but beaten between 23l and 46l over hurdles (2m); 28-1 for last month's chase debut at Warwick (2m4f, good) where she was still in touch when falling five out; there was still a long way to go but this looks an even weaker race.
runners.comment: Long way to go when falling at Warwick (chase debut) but this looks even weaker

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: No show at all in two 2m handicap hurdles following 15 months out, including returned to good ground latest; hardly a solid option, though this is her chase debut and a tongue-tie is added.
runners.comment: hardly a solid option, though this is her chase debut and a tongue-tie is added.

runners.name: Auntie Maggie
runners.spotlight: Won three handicap hurdles within the space of four runs last year (all at 2m on goodish ground) and the last of those was off this mark; may have needed the run last time after a break and showed some aptitude for fences when an 18l third at Uttoxeter in October.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins; showed some aptitude for fences when 18l third at Uttoxeter in October

runners.name: Feet On The Ground
runners.spotlight: Ultimately disappointing over hurdles (0-12) but she has some competitive form off this sort of mark and a pointing background bodes well for this switch to fences.
runners.comment: Modest hurdler but a pointing background bodes well for this switch to fences

races.race_name: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Masterdream
runners.spotlight: Won three handicap chases last season (one over C&D on good) and back on his last winning mark; however, he's finished a remote last in his final two chase starts.
runners.comment: Capable chaser off this mark but he's finished a remote last in his final two chase starts

runners.name: Impatient
runners.spotlight: Won in fine style at Huntingdon (2m7f, good to soft) in December and went close at Market Rasen (3m, soft) next time; has suffered a heavy defeat since then but a break could have done him good.
runners.comment: In good form until heavy defeat last time; a break could have done him good

runners.name: Sunray Shadow
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had a lot of racing and he makes his chase debut off the same mark he defied fairly comfortably in a 3m1f handicap hurdle last week at Huntingdon (good to firm); major claims if he takes to his new discipline.
runners.comment: Makes chase debut off the same mark he defied comfortably last week over hurdles
runners.owner: S T Racing - The Founders' Syndicate

runners.name: Zacony Rebel
runners.spotlight: Won from well out of the weights at Ludlow and off only 3lb higher he followed up at the expense of three rivals (3m, good), taking his record there to 3-5; has another 6lb rise to contend with but has won here and has to be taken seriously.
runners.comment: Thriving 11yo who has gone back-to-back at Ludlow and he's won here before

runners.name: Court In A Storm
runners.spotlight: Confined to good ground for all eight races; a bumper and two-time novice hurdle winner who has been running well in staying handicap hurdles and he may well improve for going chasing, as did his useful dam.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler who may well improve for going chasing, as did his useful dam

runners.name: Mt Fugi Park
runners.spotlight: Seemed to be running well in first-time blinkers before falling at Carlisle and he then made all the running under Gina Andrews at Market Rasen (3m, good); up 4lb but the blinkers have made a difference and should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Up 4lb but the blinkers have made a difference and should remain competitive

runners.name: Samuel Spade
runners.spotlight: Last season's light campaign over hurdles did not go well (tried in cheekpieces latterly) but he shouldn't mind getting back on drier ground and is too well handicapped to dismiss on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Quiet last season but too well handicapped to dismiss on chasing debut

races.race_name: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: King's Scholar
runners.spotlight: Useful three-time Flat winner (1m4f/1m5f) and was consistent until getting too high in the weights; his Ludlow run from off the pace was encouraging and he then won comfortably under a more forward ride at Southwell (2m, good; first-time tongue-tie); carries a penalty but has clear claims.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat and won well at Southwell; clear claims under a penalty
runners.owner: Paddy Brennan Racing Syndicate King S

runners.name: So Proud
runners.spotlight: Bought for £100,000 after winning a Haydock bumper for Don Cantillon; beaten a shade over 20l in two maiden hurdles (good to soft/good) but disappointing if he can't raise his game sooner or later.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but beaten a shade over 20l in two maiden hurdles; should do better

runners.name: Arbitration
runners.spotlight: Won over 9.4f on the AW last September for Karl Burke and bought for 67,000gns at the sales; the hood could be a significant addition as he pulled way too hard when beaten 9l behind the favourite at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good).
runners.comment: Flat winner; second on hurdle debut despite pulling hard; now hooded

runners.name: Delta Legend
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly on AW, winning twice in 2024 then eventually regressing; 100-1 when pulled up on his recent hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Regressive on the Flat and 100-1 when pulled up on his recent hurdle debut

runners.name: Nannymooresbridge
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in two bumpers and as many runs over hurdles.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in two bumpers and as many runs over hurdles

runners.name: Magna Patch
runners.spotlight: Only a smidgen of promise in three runs over hurdles and handicaps will be more like it.
runners.comment: Only a smidgen of promise in three runs over hurdles and handicaps will be more like it

races.race_name: Safer Gambling At CopyBet Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Son Of Tyran
runners.spotlight: Second in a 3m point so no major surprise to see him improve for the step up to 3m (from 2m) when justifying favouritism at Ludlow (good); raised 5lb but it could be onwards and upwards.
runners.comment: Raised a mile in trip when narrowly successful over Ludlow's 3m; 5lb higher now

runners.name: Brave Move
runners.spotlight: Has to bounce back from a poor run at Hereford but prior to that he wasn't far away over 3m at Taunton (good); he's up to winning a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Poor last time but prior to that he wasn't far away over 3m at Taunton

runners.name: Whenthemusicstops
runners.spotlight: Weak 16-1 chance on handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) and never counted; cheekpieces are added.
runners.comment: No much substance to his form and tailed off on handicap debut; cheekpieces added
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

runners.name: Saint Vic
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler but has twice threatened in handicaps, last time going close over Uttoxeter's 2m7f; that was his second run after a lengthy break; up 4lb.
runners.comment: Has twice threatened in handicaps, last time going close over Uttoxeter's 2m7f

runners.name: Artiste d'Ainay
runners.spotlight: Won a point before joining this yard for £10,000; ran well for a long way at Hexham before weakening (2m7f, good); this flatter track could suit and Sean Bowen is booked.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way at Hexham before weakening; flatter track here

runners.name: Edna E Mode
runners.spotlight: Has placed form but she's now 0-17 after finishing well behind Son Of Tyran at Ludlow.
runners.comment: Has placed form but she's now 0-17 after finishing well behind Son Of Tyran at Ludlow

runners.name: Mexican Stan
runners.spotlight: Hadn't improved for contesting handicaps but that changed when upped to 3m1f at Warwick (good) where he was well backed; didn't win with loads in hand but only went up 4lb.
runners.comment: Successful on the step up to 3m1f at Warwick where he was well backed

runners.name: Jubertilee
runners.spotlight: Questionable what he's achieved in his recent placings but up in trip for this handicap debut and he won six times in points; a possible improver, for all that he's a 9yo.
runners.comment: He's up in trip for this handicap debut and he won six times in points

runners.name: Mc'flo
runners.spotlight: Has yet to show any notable form over hurdles, including in three handicaps; now tongue tied.
runners.comment: Has yet to show notable form over hurdles, including in three handicaps; now tongue tied

runners.name: Brionywells
runners.spotlight: Things appeared to be picking up when fourth at Uttoxeter (stepped up to 2m4f, good) but she then disappointed over a tad further at Sedgefield; hard to be confident.
runners.comment: The Uttoxeter fourth was better but she then disappointed at Sedgefield

runners.name: Ultra Viers
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who has been struggling over fences and he's out of the weights here.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who has been struggling over fences and he's out of the weights here

races.race_name: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Charging Thunder
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who is 0-4 in handicaps; however, he should be on a fair mark given the level of his Flat ability and he ran quite well in a valuable Newmarket handicap at the Guineas meeting; player.
runners.comment: 2-6 over hurdles; last time he ran well in a valuable handicap at Newmarket

runners.name: Cawthorne Banker
runners.spotlight: Won twice in the space of three starts last year but has soon returned to the doldrums; can't be supported with any confidence.
runners.comment: Won twice in the space of three starts last year but has soon returned to the doldrums

runners.name: Fidendum
runners.spotlight: Has his quirks (ran out two runs back) but he made all to floor a subsequent Skelton winner in a 2m maiden at Market Rasen (good) and enters handicaps with an unexposed profile; respected.
runners.comment: Has some quirks but made all in a maiden last time; unexposed handicap debutant

runners.name: Scintillante
runners.spotlight: Won twice here last summer and some sign of a return to form when fourth at Warwick last week; this mark isn't a gift but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Won twice here last summer and some sign of a return to form when fourth at Warwick

runners.name: Get The Value
runners.spotlight: Exposed 8yo who has been out of sorts in five runs following a break; wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Exposed 8yo who has been out of sorts in five runs following a break

runners.name: Siam Park
runners.spotlight: 4-9 over fences but has departed mid-race in his last two chases and now reverts to hurdling off a 5lb lower mark; 0-10 in this discipline but he has been placed and could go well.
runners.comment: 4-9 over fences but has departed mid-race in his last two chases; lower hurdle mark

runners.name: Crown Of India
runners.spotlight: Won a good-ground maiden here last June but hasn't offered much in five handicaps subsequently; his mark is dropping if nothing else.
runners.comment: Hasn't offered much in five handicaps since winning a maiden here last summer

races.race_name: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Playful Fox
runners.spotlight: Signs of progress until unseating two out at Uttoxeter (2m, good) and backed that up with a second here last week; half-brother to the stable's smart 2m-2m6f winner Unexpected Party, so this trip should be well within range.
runners.comment: Signs of progress over 2m and this longer distance should be well within range

runners.name: Churchman
runners.spotlight: Bang there in 2m3f/2m6f handicaps (good) in March and feasible excuses since then, as he was pressured up front two runs back and perhaps failed to stay 3m last time; now 0-23 under rules but one to bear in mind.
runners.comment: Bang there in 2m3f/2m6f handicaps (good) in March and feasible excuses since then

runners.name: Mancero
runners.spotlight: French hurdles winner; looks handicapped about right on recent exploits but entitled to be thereabouts once more off his light weight and with Sean Bowen booked.
runners.comment: Entitled to be thereabouts once more off his light weight and with Sean Bowen booked

runners.name: Man Oh Man
runners.spotlight: In this headgear combination for his last three races and went close in the penultimate one at Exeter (2m7f, good); consistency is an issue but has a big run in him.
runners.comment: Consistency is an issue but has a big run in him, as penultimate effort confirms

runners.name: Greer Hill
runners.spotlight: Hasn't raised his modest profile in two handicaps, pulling up in both; the tongue-tie tried last time is joined by cheekpieces; risky.
runners.comment: Hasn't raised his modest profile in two handicaps, pulling up in both

runners.name: Georgia's Charm
runners.spotlight: Placed in two bumpers for Harry Fry; hasn't got a lot going at all for current yard, including a handicap two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Hasn't got a lot going at all for current yard, including a handicap two weeks ago

runners.name: Dontaskchris
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced for an 8yo but he was tailed off in the first-time hood on last month's handicap debut (2m3f, good; 18-1).
runners.comment: Tailed off in the first-time hood on last month's handicap debut (2m3f, good; 18-1)

runners.name: Stay Hopeful
