runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; very quiet in his first three races over hurdles and again on handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good; first-time cheekpieces), though did appear to perform below market expectations having gone off at 5-1; could be given another chance.
runners.comment: Again well beaten on handicap debut but was only 5-1; could be given another chance

runners.name: Tsavo Park
runners.spotlight: Running well when falling on his chase debut but pulled up and tailed off in his last two runs in that discipline; returned to hurdles two runs back at Bellewstown and wasn't beaten far, so could feature.
runners.comment: Returned to hurdles two runs back at Bellewstown and wasn't beaten far

runners.name: Scottish Dancer
runners.spotlight: 1-16 over hurdles and lost his way in this discipline when trained in Ireland; however, he's back on track for new yard having finished first and second in 1m6f/2m Flat handicaps this month; could be on a handy mark back over jumps.
runners.comment: Lost his way over jumps in Ireland but he's rediscovered Flat form for new yard

races.race_name: Star Sports Handicap

runners.name: Major Neigh Sayer
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Newmarket maiden (good) in October but finished down the field having led in French heavy-ground Listed race next time; weakened when attempting 1m on his comeback in April but this first start at 5f since his 2yo debut is still something of a surprise; fascinating to see what he makes of it in an additional tongue-tie with Oisin Murphy steering.
runners.comment: 7f maiden winner; weakened over 1m latest but drop to 5f still a surprise; tongue-tie on

runners.name: The Lost Sock
runners.spotlight: Catterick maiden (good) winner in August who was every bit as good when third at Wolverhampton (also 5f, AW; Kolkata Knight second) in March; didn't look to be making much of an impression when the door slammed in his face at Thirsk last time but it's too soon to write him off.
runners.comment: Little impression before he was hampered last time but it is too soon to write him off

runners.name: Killavia
runners.spotlight: Improved in three consecutive C&D appearances last summer, finishing third of seven in Listed event before a commanding maiden win (also good; made all) in July; was cast in her box the night before an August assignment after which her trainer recently reported she was never really right; that stable tour also offered hope she's back in a good place and can go well on this handicap debut/reappearance.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner who had an excuse subsequently; said to be back in good nick; player

runners.name: Gouken
runners.spotlight: In the placings on all five starts on a variety of surfaces as a juvenile (6f/7f) and he deservedly made the breakthrough on his reappearance at Newcastle (6f, AW) last month; respectable fifth of 14 at Hamilton on his recent handicap debut and this experiment at 5f looks worth a go.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth of 14 on recent handicap debut (6f); experiment at 5f looks worth a go

runners.name: Akirra
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f; fast turf no problem when runner-up at Windsor (latterly promoted to first) in August and he bolted up in Wolverhampton maiden in November having previously gone quite close on handicap debut at Southwell (also AW); underwhelmed on final start of last term but subsequently gelded and he's in the right hands to progress this season.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner who acts on fast turf; has been gelded and can progress this term

runners.name: Havana Smile
runners.spotlight: Sole start at 5f came at this track and it was his best effort too, when second of nine in a maiden (good) in August; last two runs were at 7f and didn't scream he's desperate for this return to 5f; not handicapped out of things, though.
runners.comment: Best effort when second over C&D but last two 7f starts raise doubts over the return to 5f

runners.name: Kolkata Knight
runners.spotlight: Landed Hamilton maiden (6f, good to firm) on debut but better form after wind surgery when bagging Haydock handicap (5f, good to soft) in September; subsequently gelded and ran well for second (5f, AW; winner on the middle leg of a hat-trick) on his reappearance, while latest start from a wide stall at Chester can be written off; needs considering.
runners.comment: Latest run can be written off and previously ran well on reappearance; considered

runners.name: Our Cody
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her third start when taking a Windsor maiden (good to firm) in June and it was a fine effort when second of 12 in Listed event at Ayr (also 5f, soft) in September; makes her handicap debut on this reappearance and she warrants respect.
runners.comment: Good Listed second (soft) last term and she's respected on her return/handicap debut

races.race_name: Star Sports National Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Adaay Of Scarlett
runners.spotlight: Impressive on debut at Newmarket (5f, good) last month and narrowly followed up in a warm, three-runner conditions race at Ascot (5f, good to firm); he could have improvement in him and is one to consider.
runners.comment: 2-2, the wins coming at Newmarket and Ascot, and he could have improvement in him

runners.name: Napa
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise on last month's debut fourth at Newbury (5f, good) to win valuable novice at Newmarket (5f, good to firm); did it nicely from the front last time and he's a leading contender today.
runners.comment: Did it nicely in valuable novice at Newmarket and he's a leading contender

runners.name: Princesse d'Orange
runners.spotlight: Easily made all when favourite on debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) last month; did too much too soon when attempting to repeat those tactics in Listed race at York (5f, good) a fortnight ago, finishing fifth of ten, but she's not written off in view of her initial promise.
runners.comment: Impressive on debut; unplaced in Listed race at York since but she's not written off

runners.name: A Bear Affair
runners.spotlight: Won the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) in March and beaten only 1l when third of three to Adaay Of Scarlett at Ascot four weeks ago (5f, good to firm; same terms today); something to find with the winner but might not be far away.
runners.comment: Won the Brocklesby then close third to Adaay Of Scarlett at Ascot; might not be far away
runners.owner: A Bit On The Side & Partner

runners.name: Where Love Lives
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite when readily making all at Thirsk (5f, good) last month and that form has been franked by the clear runner-up; this likeable prospect is firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Made all on debut at Thirsk and form franked by clear runner-up; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Dandyman Dan
runners.spotlight: Belied 40-1 odds and evident inexperience to win on last month's debut at Bath (5.7f, firm); likely to improve but others have more substance to their form.
runners.comment: Belied 40-1 odds and evident inexperience to win at Bath but others have stronger form
runners.owner: Eric Griffiths, Mrs T Burns & Partner

runners.name: Mr Macartney
runners.spotlight: Won at Leicester (5f, good to firm) last month on second start; faded when seventh of 13 in Newmarket novice (6f, good) and returns to 5f today, but it seems likely that his limitations will again be exposed.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester last month on second start but soundly beaten at Newmarket since

runners.name: Bill The Bull
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 15 to A Bear Affair in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) and again showed promise when third at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) recently; having hung right last time he could appreciate having a rail on his right-hand side today, but others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Promise on both starts and should have more to offer but others have stronger claims

races.race_name: Star Sports Henry II Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Sweet William
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2024 and has added three more Group-race wins, the latest on reappearance in this month's Sagaro Stakes at Ascot (2m, good to firm) where he was clear with subsequent Group 1 winner Caballo De Mar; leading claims despite 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 and form of Sagaro Stakes win has already been franked; leading claims

runners.name: Lazy Griff
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Derby at Epsom (1m4f, good) last June and kept on well for third in the Irish Derby at the Curragh (1m4f, good) later that month; absent since but this lightly raced 4yo could be suited by the step up in trip and he's a major player if ready to rumble after his absence.
runners.comment: Placed in the Derby and Irish Derby last June; absent since but could be a major player

runners.name: Furthur
runners.spotlight: He hasn't kicked on since beating Epic Poet to win the Group 3 Geoffrey Freer at Newbury (1m5f, good to firm) last August and failed to threaten when fourth in the Yorkshire Cup at York three weeks ago; however, he could improve for that first outing since finishing mid-division in the Melbourne Cup (gelded between those starts); this 4yo remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on since last August's Geoffrey Freer win but still one to be interested in

runners.name: Paradias
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has a good record here and has been better than ever in AW handicaps this year, and he wasn't beaten far when fifth of six in Group 2 Jockey Club Stakes at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) four weeks ago; however, his form needs to go to a new level and he's unraced beyond 1m4f on the Flat.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when fifth in Jockey Club Stakes but form needs to go to a new level today

runners.name: Epic Poet
runners.spotlight: Beaten in Group races on his last ten starts but went close in the Yorkshire Cup at York (1m6f, good to firm) last May and he was a good third in that same Group 2 a fortnight ago (good); he should be fine back up in trip and might not be far away.
runners.comment: Yet to win a Group race but third in the Yorkshire Cup last time and might not be far away

runners.name: Duke Of Oxford
runners.spotlight: Progressive on AW in the winter with 2m handicap wins at Kempton and Newcastle; however, only fifth of 12 at Newcastle (2m) last month and the suitability of turf is questionable.
runners.comment: Progressive in AW h'caps in the winter but soundly beaten last time and unproven on turf
runners.owner: A Cope, B Roberts And J Biggane

runners.name: Dubai Future
runners.spotlight: Won Group 3 (1m6f, good) and Group 2 (2m, good) races at Meydan early last year and he's very much of interest on that form; however, not at the same level subsequently and he was a soundly beaten fourth behind Sweet William in the Group 3 Sagaro at Ascot (2m, good to firm) recently.
runners.comment: Group 3 and Group 2 wins at Meydan early last year but not at the same level subsequently

races.race_name: Star Sports Heron Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Yazin
runners.spotlight: Two novice wins last autumn (7f, good to soft/good) and he comfortably completed the hat-trick when making all in a warm, three-runner conditions race at Southwell (1m, AW) on last month's reappearance; in top hands (one of two for the yard) and he could take this rise in grade in his stride.
runners.comment: He's won his last three and could take this rise in grade in his stride

runners.name: Talk Of New York
runners.spotlight: Impressive at Kempton (1m, AW) last October on sole 2yo start and returned with close Listed third at Meydan (1m, good) before comfortable conditions race win at Newmarket (7f, good) where he settled better in a first-time hood (retained); that latest form hasn't worked out well but he is William Buick's mount of the four Godolphin runners and is a key player.
runners.comment: Conditions race win at Newmarket latest; Buick's mount of the Godolphin four; key player

runners.name: Wise Prince
runners.spotlight: Promising win at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) last October on sole 2yo start and showed up very well before fading into third in the Group 3 Classic Trial here (1m2f, good) on his reappearance; pulled hard and weakened when finishing last in the Group 2 Dante at York (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; this drop back in trip looks the right move and he retains potential.
runners.comment: He's weakened in Derby trials over 1m2f; this drop back in trip looks the right move

runners.name: Time To Turn
runners.spotlight: Made it three wins from his six 2yo starts when landing the Group 3 Horris Hill at Newbury (7f, soft) last October; has also won on good ground; carries a 5lb penalty on his reappearance and Talk Of New York may be his top yard's chief hope, but he won with something to spare at Newbury and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Won Group 3 Horris Hill last October; penalised on reappearance; could have part to play

runners.name: Andab
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Dundalk (7f, AW) last October and running well in defeat this year, particularly at the Curragh (1m, good; Listed) this month when 2l runner-up in first-time cheekpieces (retained); with a clear run he'd have finished closer last time and holds each-way claims under a 3lb penalty.
runners.comment: AW Listed winner last October; Listed runner-up at the Curragh last time; each-way claims

runners.name: Maximized
runners.spotlight: Won last May/June on first two starts and some creditable runs since, but he's finished held at Newmarket on both outings this spring (7f, good/good to firm), including behind stablemate Talk Of New York; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won last May/June on first two starts but opposable on what he's been showing this spring

runners.name: Nation's Hope
runners.spotlight: 5l win on debut at Kempton (1m, AW) last November and made all by 6l in novice there (1m) the following month; takes a steep rise in grade this evening and most of today's rivals have greater substance to their form, including two of his stablemates, but it remains to be seen where his limitations lie and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Easy wins on both starts; steep rise in grade but remains to be seen where limitations lie

runners.name: Golden Knight
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on debut at Haydock (1m, good to soft) last September and built on that to make all by nearly 2l at Newmarket (1m, good) in October; unexposed and could have more to offer but has plenty to find today.
runners.comment: Made all at Newmarket last October on second run; unexposed but others have stronger form

races.race_name: Star Sports Brigadier Gerard Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Ombudsman
runners.spotlight: Second in this last year on his reappearance and he's won three Group 1 races since (1m2f/1m1f, good to firm/good), the latest the Dubai Turf at Meydan in March on his sole outing this year; John Gosden has said that this is "going to bring him on to where we want him at (Royal) Ascot" but he'll be very tough to beat under a 7lb penalty if close to the top of his game.
runners.comment: 3-time Group 1 winner; very tough to beat under penalty if close to the top of his game

runners.name: Almeric
runners.spotlight: Three wins (1m-1m2f, soft/good to soft) from his first four starts, including two Listed races; his progress has stalled on good going on his two starts since but one was the Group 1 Champion Stakes and the other was last month's reappearance (here); it's too soon to label him a slow-ground specialist and he's not written off in view of his abundant earlier promise.
runners.comment: Looked classy prospect last September; progress has stalled since but he's not written off

runners.name: Gethin
runners.spotlight: 2-2 (heavy/good) prior to finishing second in two Listed races last autumn (1m4f/1m2f, very soft/good to soft); reappeared with convincing Listed win at Kempton (1m2f, AW), an improved performance which bodes very well for his future prospects; acquired by Wathnan Racing since that last run; respected.
runners.comment: Reappeared with a convincing Listed win at Kempton; lightly raced and respected

runners.name: Bedouin Prince
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 4yo who went close in a handicap at Meydan in January (1m2f, good; behind Arabian Light) and did likewise in a Listed race in Bahrain (1m3f, good to firm) in February; however, further improvement is required if he's to make a serious impact this evening.
runners.comment: Went close in UAE and Bahrain early this year but further improvement is required

runners.name: Arabian Light
runners.spotlight: Beat Bedouin Prince to win handicap at Meydan (1m2f, good) in January and very close second in Group 3 there (1m2f, good) later that month; this 5yo has had just ten starts and further improvement cannot be ruled out, but a career best is necessary if he's to come out on top today.
runners.comment: Solid form at Meydan in January but career best is needed if he's to come out on top today

runners.name: Wimbledon Hawkeye
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent in defeat in Group races last year before winning the Grade 3 Nashville Derby at Kentucky (10.5f, firm) last August; well beaten in the Breeders' Cup Turf next time; has an each-way shout if fully tuned up for this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner at Kentucky last August; each-way shout if fully tuned up on reappearance

races.race_name: Starsports.Bet Whitsun Cup Handicap

runners.name: Boyfriend
runners.spotlight: Consistent across his five starts last year and won off this mark at Nottingham (1m, good) last June; reappeared with close eighth of 24 in the Spring Cup at Newbury (1m, good) and could build on that effort today; chance.
runners.comment: Reappeared with close eighth of 24 in the Spring Cup and could build on that effort

runners.name: Cogitate
runners.spotlight: He needs to produce more than he's managed back on turf on his last two starts but prior to those he was a good second at Kempton (7f, AW) in March, and he won on grass at Salisbury (1m, good to firm) last May; capable of having a say if getting back to his best.
runners.comment: Mid-division on last two starts but capable of having a say if getting back to his best

runners.name: Raammee
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Kempton (1m, AW) last August and easily justified 1-9 odds in a novice at Newcastle (1m, AW) in September; his opening mark is a lofty one but he has the potential to be better than it; respected on return.
runners.comment: 2-2 last August/September; lofty opening mark but has the potential to be better than it

runners.name: Saytarr
runners.spotlight: He won earlier this year on his first three starts for James Horton (7f/1m, AW); made it 0-9 on turf when 2l third of 11 at Ascot (1m, good to firm) four weeks ago but that was still a positive run and he could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Won on AW on first three starts for yard; third at Ascot since and could be in the mix

runners.name: Dark Tornado
runners.spotlight: There was no return to his peak form on his recent stable and seasonal debut at Newmarket (7f, good) but it wasn't a bad performance and he could improve for that run; might not be far away; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly on recent stable/seasonal debut at Newmarket; could improve for that run

runners.name: Hickory
runners.spotlight: Third in this last year and improved form later in the season, finishing his campaign in fine form (on various ground); down the field at Ascot on recent return but he was hampered and may have needed the run; not discounted.
runners.comment: Finished last season in fine form and he may have needed his reappearance run at Ascot

runners.name: Nostrum
runners.spotlight: Group 3 runner-up in June 2024; nothing like the same force subsequently but some encouragement can be taken from his stable/seasonal debut in the Victoria Cup at Ascot, where he was soundly beaten overall but was first of seven in the group that raced initially on the far side; he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: He's been nothing like the force of old but this month's stable debut was encouraging

races.race_name: Starsports.Bet Handicap

runners.name: Suddenly I See
runners.spotlight: Signed off last term by landing a Musselburgh maiden (7f) and reappeared at that track when making a winning handicap debut (1m, also good) in April; raised to this mark when only sixth at Beverley next time but softer conditions were a plausible excuse; not dismissed back on a sounder surface with William Buick taking over.
runners.comment: Underwhelming last time but good to soft may not have suited; not dismissed under Buick

runners.name: The Joker
runners.spotlight: 0-6 record includes three runs here but he's run well on all of those visits, including when third of six in a hot Class 4 on his April return; filled that position again at Newmarket (also 1m2f, good) 12 days ago and this free-going sort may benefit from today's drop back to 1m; player.
runners.comment: 0-6 but has some notable course form to his name and drop back to 1m might suit; player

runners.name: Crimson Rambler
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW within five days in March and carried on his progression back on turf when second of 12 at Nottingham (also about 1m, good) in April; might have found 1m2f a slight stretch when third on his final start for George Scott and subsequently secured for 20,000gns; he's back at 1m but up in grade for this stable debut.
runners.comment: Progressive at a lower level and will probably be happier back at 1m; stable debut

runners.name: Luzon Heights
runners.spotlight: Picked up a Southwell nursery (AW) in September and after a solid comeback at Newmarket in April he posted his best effort yet when second at Ascot (also 1m, good) three weeks ago; improving colt who is much respected.
runners.comment: Best effort when second in similar event last time; improving colt who is much respected

runners.name: Loblolly
runners.spotlight: Gained due reward for a couple of close calls when making all for a convincing win at Lingfield (7f, AW) in September; beaten around 17l on his next two starts, however, including in a warm event at Southwell on his April return (had been gelded); needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Beaten around 17l on his last two starts and needs to get back on track

runners.name: Law Court
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but he's been in the frame all four starts and ran well when second at Newbury (1m, good) on his handicap debut 12 days ago; made his bid on a part of the track that was possibly unfavoured that day and looks sure to make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Solid second on handicap debut 12 days ago; run can possibly be upgraded too; contender

runners.name: Darzah
runners.spotlight: Her debut fifth at Newmarket (good) was a promising effort in April and she had little difficulty getting off the mark when scoring at Bath (also 1m, firm) a fortnight ago; still a touch raw that day and she brings plenty of potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Comfortably off the mark on second start; brings potential to her handicap debut

runners.name: Blues And Royals
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner this year but he's fine on turf too and ran creditably when second of ten at Musselburgh (again 1m, good) in April; up 5lb for latest Kempton success three weeks ago but likely he can do better again.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW and turf and likely he can do better again despite 5lb rise
runners.owner: D Fish, S Jones & J Barnett

runners.name: Trio
runners.spotlight: Leicester maiden (good to soft) winner in October but she's been stuck in third place since, including when she came up against Blues And Royals at Kempton (again 1m, AW) three weeks ago; should give her running while finding one or two stronger again.
runners.comment: Has been stuck in third place and likely to find one or two stronger again
runners.owner: M H Dixon & J L Rowsell

runners.name: Montague Menace
runners.spotlight: Took a 7f Lingfield maiden (AW) on his comeback in March but bombed out when strong in the market on his handicap debut at Epsom (8.5f, good) next time; finished 12th of 14 at York a fortnight ago and has a point to prove now.
runners.comment: Disappointed on his last two starts and has a point to prove now

runners.name: Brave Hunter
runners.spotlight: Made a big move at halfway but stuck to his task when second of 12 at Windsor (1m, good to firm; winner was completing a four-timer) 17 days ago; that was his handicap debut and first go at 1m so plenty of optimism he can progress again with Oisin Murphy taking over.
runners.comment: Good second last time on handicap debut; can likely progress again and he's feared

runners.name: Brighlee
runners.spotlight: Chased home a subsequent Listed winner at Salisbury (1m, good to firm) in August but disappointed in October after a break; plugged on without threatening when fifth at Nottingham on her reappearance in April and she'll need to tap back into that Salisbury promise to make an impact on this handicap debut; newly fitted hood might help, though.
runners.comment: Good second in August hasn't been repeated; tries a hood for this handicap debut

races.race_name: Daredevil Events Adrenaline Stunt Circus Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Luker's Tipple
runners.spotlight: Second in seven of his 12 races over hurdles, including when beaten 16l by subsequent Grade 1 winner Zeus Power at Thurles last December; had a Flat run last month; overdue for a win and looks the part here despite his concerning profile.
runners.comment: Second in seven of his 12 races over hurdles, had a Flat run last month, overdue a win

runners.name: Game Point
runners.spotlight: Useful but frustrating for Ger Lyons on the Flat, runner-up five times in a row in maidens, rated 77; fair third on hurdling debut at Sligo; good chance of reaching the money.
runners.comment: A five-time runner-up on the Flat for Ger Lyons, third on hurdling debut, good chance

runners.name: Lureka Du Noyer
runners.spotlight: Placed over fences in France; has plenty of experience; likely to make his presence felt in a race with little depth.
runners.comment: Placed over fences in France, has plenty of experience, likely to make his presence felt

runners.name: Adifferentballowax
runners.spotlight: From a good French jumping family; made his debut in a strong bumper at the Punchestown festival; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, made his debut in a strong bumper at Punchestown, decent pedigree
runners.owner: John F Doyle & R Bannerman & Mrs Sheila O'Ryan

runners.name: Ballyminnion Boy
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete his two point-to-point starts, not an obvious contender.
runners.comment: Failed to complete his two point-to-point starts, not an obvious contender

runners.name: Great Entirely
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a point; sixth of eight on hurdling debut at Wexford last month; considerable improvement needed.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a point, sixth of eight on hurdling debut, considerable improvement needed

runners.name: Million Dollar Man
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on sole point run; has a fair pedigree and emerged with credit with sixth placing at 200-1 on recent hurdling debut at Clonmel.
runners.comment: Emerged with credit with sixth placing at 200-1 on recent hurdling debut at Clonmel

runners.name: Drumadeise
runners.spotlight: Of some interest as a half-sister to Flat/bumper/hurdle winner Getaway Katie Mai; trainer did well with Master McShee some years ago.
runners.comment: Of some interest as a half-sister to Flat/bumper/hurdle winner Getaway Katie Mai

runners.name: Euro Agent
runners.spotlight: Mild promise on second bumper outing for Eoin Griffin, none too fluent and ran out before last on hurdles debut at Leopardstown; remote fifth at Tramore last month on stable debut; does not seem straightforward; others preferred.
runners.comment: Remote fifth at Tramore last month, does not seem straightforward, others preferred

runners.name: Tellyouwhat
runners.spotlight: Kingston Hill gelding, dam showed little but is a half-sister to several winners; fitted with tongue-tie for debut.
runners.comment: Kingston Hill gelding, dam showed little but is a half-sister to several winners

runners.name: Inchidaly Rua
runners.spotlight: Fair runs at Tramore and Cork; not a likely winner here, but could be one for handicaps in time.
runners.comment: Fair runs at Tramore and Cork, could be one for handicaps in time

runners.name: Lincoln Mill
runners.spotlight: Clear of the third when runner-up in a maiden point at Dromahane recently; may need further; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, clear of the third when runner-up in a maiden point, may need further

runners.name: Lucky Vick
runners.spotlight: Fair effort at Thurles in his first bumper; not as good at Cork next time; best watched on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Fair effort in his first bumper, not as good next time, best watched on hurdling debut

runners.name: Another Blunder
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two bumpers last year; same on hurdling debut at Clonmel recently.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two bumpers last year, same on hurdling debut at Clonmel recently

runners.name: Kidam Roque
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two bumpers; weak form on his last three Dundalk visits after hints of ability on his first two runs at that venue; no hurdling experience.
runners.comment: Weak form on his last three Dundalk visits after hints of ability on his first two AW runs

runners.name: Denham Rock
runners.spotlight: Well held by Game Point on running at Sligo recently; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, well held by Game Point on running at Sligo recently

runners.name: King Maxios
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in 2m4f maidens at Wexford and Killarney; in good hands but of no interest at this early stage of his career.
runners.comment: Tailed off in 2m4f maidens at Wexford and Killarney, of no interest at this early stage

runners.name: Carry On Star
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on both starts in points last October; same outcome in a maiden hurdle at Cork.
runners.comment: Pulled up on both starts in points last October, same outcome in a maiden hurdle at Cork

runners.name: God The Highness
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in low-grade staying handicaps on the Flat; rated 19lb inferior to Game Point; beaten in excess of 60l on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability in low-grade staying handicaps on the Flat, early days hurdling

runners.name: Seekthisfairland
runners.spotlight: In rear in maiden hurdles at Wexford and Kilbeggan; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: In rear in maiden hurdles at Wexford and Kilbeggan, safe to rule out
runners.owner: Kevin Sexton & James McIlwee & James Convery & M G Byrne

races.race_name: Great National South Court Hotel And Dial A Bus Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: La Cote Fleurie
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite when winning fair Tipperary bumper (2m, good to yielding) on debut in October; returned from seven-month break when beaten 1.75l into third of 15 on Cork hurdles debut (2m3f, good) 18 days ago; high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Beaten 1.75l into third of 15 on Cork hurdles debut (2m3f, good); high on the shortlist

runners.name: Aruntothequeen
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper winner at Gowran in 2024; decent efforts in maidens at 2m-2m4f; has been found wanting in handicaps, including valuable mares' race at the DRF; had a big ask at the weights when third to Switch From Diesel at Punchestown on latest (13lb worse than handicap terms); contender.
runners.comment: Big ask at the weights when third to Switch From Diesel at Punchestown on latest; player
runners.owner: J P O'Flaherty & Mark E Phelan

runners.name: Carleys Lilly
runners.spotlight: Cheaply bought Blue Bresil mare but eyecatching debut effort when never-nearer fourth of 18 at Clonmel (16.5f, good to yielding) a fortnight ago, looking in need of this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Eyecatching debut effort when never-nearer fourth of 18 at Clonmel; this trip can suit

runners.name: Ardeen Joy
runners.spotlight: First two runs at Kilbeggan and Ballinrobe offered little but outran 125-1 odds when remote third of 14 in 2m3f Cork mares' maiden last month; Sam Ewing takes over from claimer; likely improver.
runners.comment: Outran 125-1 odds when remote third of 14 in 2m3f Cork mares' maiden last month

runners.name: Knockarina
runners.spotlight: Solid bumper form overall, particularly the runner-up finish to Familiar Dreams at Cork in January 2024; well beaten in two course bumpers a year apart; returns from 413-day absence; betting will provide clues on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Solid bumper form overall but returns from lengthy absence; hurdles debut; watch market

runners.name: Pixie Hill
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to 2m1f hurdle winner Getaway Henry; dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Warrantor and Thebarrowman, out of bumper winner; pulled up sole point outing.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 2m1f hurdle winner Getaway Henry; pulled up sole point outing

runners.name: Chisel Hill
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at Punchestown on debut; 52l behind Aruntothequeen when fourth at that track on latest; up against it.
runners.comment: Finished 52l behind Aruntothequeen when fourth at Punchestown on latest; up against it

runners.name: Becca Sommers
runners.spotlight: Maxios mare; half-sister to five winners inc Jaime Sommers (2m Flat, RPR 66; useful 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase), Pairc Na Ngael (2m4f-3m1f chase) and Halsafari (2m-2m4f hurdle/chase); dam very tough 1m6f-2m Flat and high-class bumper/2m-2m6f hurdle winner; market can guide.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 5 winners; dam very tough 1m6f-2m Flat and high-class bumper/hurdle winner

runners.name: Certosa
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in four bumpers and maiden hurdle; best to look elsewhere on this occasion.
runners.comment: Well beaten in four bumpers and maiden hurdle; best to look elsewhere on this occasion

runners.name: Humble Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Berkshire mare; dam unraced half-sister to 1m6f Flat and smart 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Starchitect, out of 1m2f Listed winner; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to 1m6f Flat and smart 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Starchitect

runners.name: Jessie Roe
runners.spotlight: Five bumper efforts have amounted to little and a similar tale in her points; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Five bumper efforts have amounted to little and a similar tale in points; hurdles debut

runners.name: Doesthatmakesense
runners.spotlight: Sent off at odds of 33-1 when tailed off on bumper debut at Killarney this month; others preferred.
runners.comment: Sent off at odds of 33-1 when tailed off on bumper debut at Killarney this month

runners.name: Eyecanboogie
runners.spotlight: Remote third in bumper for Willie Mullins; tailed off on stable debut; no impression on hurdling bow in tongue-tie (disgarded); needs to find major improvement but worth a market check nonetheless for local trainer.
runners.comment: Needs to find major improvement but worth a market check nonetheless for local trainer

runners.name: Tillyiver
runners.spotlight: Poor form thus far and completely tailed off behind La Cote Fleurie at Cork on return; hooded.
runners.comment: Poor form thus far & completely tailed off behind La Cote Fleurie at Cork on return; hood

runners.name: An Bhfuil Tu Reidh
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in points; tailed off on rules debut at Killarney this month; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Well beaten in points; tailed off on rules debut at Killarney this month; safe to rule out

runners.name: Tawny Lea
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to smart 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Duke Of Navan but always in rear on debut at Sligo 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Half-sister to smart winner Duke Of Navan but always in rear on debut at Sligo

races.race_name: Irish Wire Products Ltd Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Whitewinewednesday
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; a staying-on third at Navan last September (2m3f, yielding) was her best run for Matt Daly; failed to build on that on her last two starts before a break; down the field at Clonmel last month; an intriguing amateur-ridden Closutton runner now.
runners.comment: Point winner, showed some ability over hurdles for Matt Daly, intriguing Closutton runner

runners.name: Amburana
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame twice at Tramore; beaten 20l in third on penultimate start; previous fourth was better, not beaten far in a race very well advertised by the winner Pebble Bleu and third-placed Cousin Kate; probably found 2m too sharp at Punchestown; each-way prospect.
runners.comment: Probably found 2m too sharp at Punchestown, previous Tramore runs advertise her chance

runners.name: Gaoithe Glory
runners.spotlight: Second in a Listowel bumper (soft) in 2024 on debut; ordinary hurdles form overall; fourth over 2m here six weeks ago was reasonably promising after a break; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Fourth over 2m here was reasonably promising after a break, one for the shortlist

runners.name: Let Him B
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicap hurdler in March/April; off the mark over 2m5f at Down Royal (yielding) and better again off 11lb higher when clear second to a ready winner over C&D (soft-to-heavy); every chance here if a poor recent chase run is ignored; reservations about good ground.
runners.comment: Every chance here if a poor recent chase run is ignored, reservations about good ground

runners.name: Larkins Lane
runners.spotlight: Fifth at Clonmel in March was slightly promising; two less satisfactory runs since then at Tramore and Cork.
runners.comment: Fifth at Clonmel in March was slightly promising, two less satisfactory runs since then

runners.name: Run For The Border
runners.spotlight: Probably not suited by testing ground in two runs before a break; won a maiden on good-to-yielding over 2m3f at this venue last October.
runners.comment: Probably not suited by testing ground on last two runs, course winner on good-to-yielding

runners.name: She'll Be Kept
runners.spotlight: Remote second on her most recent point-to-point start; placed twice over hurdles last year, including in a claimer at this venue.
runners.comment: Remote second on her most recent point-to-point start, placed twice over hurdles

runners.name: Ranting And Raven
runners.spotlight: Blinkered when placed three times in middle-distance Flat handicaps on a sound surface for Johnny Murtagh; recent Punchestown sixth was best hurdles run so far and mildly encouraging.
runners.comment: Placed on the Flat for Johnny Murtagh, recent Punchestown sixth was mildly encouraging

runners.name: Boston Breeze
runners.spotlight: Did not seem to handle the track at Bellewstown last; modest overall credentials; hood fitted now, may improve.
runners.comment: Did not seem to handle the track at Bellewstown, hood fitted now, may improve

runners.name: Ixigreczed
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in 2m3f Kilbeggan handicap nearly two years ago; fair run in first-time blinkers at Clonmel in March; below that form last time at the same venue.
runners.comment: No sign of the form that brought her a win at Kilbeggan almost two years ago

runners.name: Ballinagaine Girl
runners.spotlight: Showed little in maidens; always at the back at Tramore in her first handicap.
runners.comment: Showed little in maidens, always at the back at Tramore in her first handicap

runners.name: Sekifi
runners.spotlight: Has never reached the first three; however, might not be a lost cause judged on Bellewstown sixth, not beaten far on first run after a break.
runners.comment: Has never reached the first three, might not be a lost cause judged on Bellewstown sixth

runners.name: Lake Okeechobee
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; last of 16 at Clonmel in her first handicap; cheekpieces tried now.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, last of 16 at Clonmel in her first handicap, cheekpieces tried now

runners.name: Jollie Cosmos
runners.spotlight: Placed seven times in points; modest form on the track and getting on in years now.
runners.comment: Placed seven times in points, modest form on the track, getting on in years now

runners.name: Winter Carnival
runners.spotlight: Third at this venue in January 2025 seems to have been a flash in the pan; down the field here last December and absent since; now a 22-race maiden.
runners.comment: Third at this venue in 2025 seems to have been a flash in the pan, now a 22-race maiden

runners.name: Nashville Notions
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo maiden over 2m2f last summer; unconvincing form in handicaps; reserve.
runners.comment: Reserve, won a maiden hurdle at Sligo last summer, unconvincing form in handicaps

runners.name: Thirsty Work
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; plenty of improvement needed from handicap debut at this venue.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, plenty of improvement needed from handicap debut at this venue

runners.name: Lost Features
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens; 50-1 when down the field at Wexford 12 days ago in her first handicap.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in maidens, 50-1 when down the field at Wexford in her first handicap
runners.owner: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club

runners.name: Wings Of Africa
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing in three maidens last season; a little better at Clonmel after a break; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Showed nothing in three maidens last season, a little better at Clonmel after a break

races.race_name: Irish Wire Products Ltd Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Evelyn
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame on her two most recent starts in maidens; likely to be competitive here if running to a similar level of form.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on her two most recent starts in maidens, likely to be competitive here

runners.name: Hob's Angel
runners.spotlight: 10yo mare; opened her hurdles account at Clonmel two weeks ago at the 16th attempt; 6lb higher, possibly vulnerable to younger rivals.
runners.comment: Opened her hurdles account at Clonmel at the 16th attempt, 6lb higher possibly vulnerable

runners.name: Georginas Jet
runners.spotlight: Could feature in this grade if recapturing the solid form she showed last August; however, plenty to prove on account of failures to complete on final start last year and on return last month.
runners.comment: Could feature in this grade if recapturing the solid form she showed last August

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: Little sign of ability in maidens; has time on his side and this longer trip may help to produce improvement.
runners.comment: Little sign of ability in maidens, this longer trip may help to produce improvement

runners.name: Hidalgo Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Frustrating sort, placed in seven of his 21 starts over hurdles; risky and probably just a place chance at best; has had a run on the Flat since a well beaten fifth over hurdles at Kilbeggan last month.
runners.comment: Frustrating sort, placed in seven of his 21 starts over hurdles, risky but place chance

runners.name: Getaway Henry
runners.spotlight: Shock winner at Wexford in 2024; best effort last season when runner-up at Thurles in October; best form has been over shorter than this.
runners.comment: Won at Wexford two years ago, second at Thurles last October offer some hope

runners.name: Celtic Crossing
runners.spotlight: Was entitled to need her comeback run at Sligo after a long absence; one fair run when fifth at Roscommon in 2024; otherwise little of note in her form.
runners.comment: Was entitled to need her comeback run at Sligo after a long absence, one fair run in 2024

runners.name: Tantrum Boy
runners.spotlight: Beaten long distances at huge odds in maidens; 25-1 when towards the back on handicap debut at Clonmel; cheekpieces fitted now.
runners.comment: 25-1 when towards the back on handicap debut at Clonmel, cheekpieces fitted now

runners.name: Qaasid
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on the Flat in Britain; 0-11 hurdling for this yard, best effort yet when runner-up in a claimer at Clonmel in February; leading when slipped up going to three out at the same venue nine weeks ago; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Five Flat wins, 0-11 hurdling, leading when slipped up going to three out at Clonmel
runners.owner: Four Fools & A Fast Horse Syndicate

runners.name: Coup d'Essay
runners.spotlight: No impact in maidens; seems a bit high in the ratings on the evidence of handicap runs here and at Kilbeggan.
runners.comment: Seems a bit high in the ratings on the evidence of handicap runs here and at Kilbeggan

runners.name: Go Avel
runners.spotlight: Off for a year before her recent Sligo outing; nothing in her form to encourage support.
runners.comment: Off for a year before her recent Sligo outing, nothing in her form to encourage support
runners.owner: Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey

runners.name: Getaway Blondie
runners.spotlight: Very weak form in previous years; prominent for a long way on stable debut at Bellewstown.
runners.comment: Very weak form in previous years, prominent for a long way on stable debut at Bellewstown

runners.name: Jetovango
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in points; fair fourth at Lisronagh last month, but pulled up at Tattersalls Farm on Sunday; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Placed twice in points, fair fourth last month but pulled up at Tattersalls Farm on Sunday

runners.name: Marian Avenue
runners.spotlight: Beaten only a neck by odds-on chance Spanish Maid over 2m at Punchestown 15 days ago; 5l clear of the third then, 5lb extra may not stop him; good ground seems to have sparked his improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten only a neck by an odds-on chance over 2m at Punchestown, 5lb extra may not stop him

runners.name: Loulouless
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when finishing in mid-division at Clonmel two weeks ago; others much preferred.
runners.comment: 20-1 when finishing in mid-division at Clonmel two weeks ago, others much preferred

runners.name: Not So Serious
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden hurdle form; not a big price for two bumper starts last August when trained by Andy Slattery; reserve.
runners.comment: Reserve, weak maiden hurdle form, not a big price for two bumper starts last August

runners.name: Backinabit
runners.spotlight: Ran well at Roscommon in September after a layoff; flopped when favourite at Wexford when well fancied; tailed off on final start; has changed stable; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Flopped when favourite at Wexford on penultimate start last year, has changed stable

runners.name: Calm Flight Ahead
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; no apparent chance unless cheekpieces make a big difference.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, no apparent chance unless cheekpieces make a big difference
runners.owner: Miss Phidelma Elvin & Donal P Keane

runners.name: Friar Hogan
runners.spotlight: Still early days but his chance is not obvious based on his first two handicap attempts at Thurles and Sligo.
runners.comment: Still early days but his chance is not obvious based on his first two handicap attempts

races.race_name: Easyfix Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Minella Diamond
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win at the eighth attempt over hurdles in Clonmel maiden on latest (19.5f, gd-yld); respected on handicap debut off what could be a workable mark.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant, Clonmel maiden win latest, mark could be workable, respected

runners.name: Marlpark
runners.spotlight: Sole success a maiden hurdle win at Clonmel in October 2024; ran respectably back there in March (2m3f, soft) when an 8l second of 15; tailed off twice since; revival needed.
runners.comment: Tailed off twice since respectable Clonmel run in March, needs to revive

runners.name: Cocovic
runners.spotlight: Point winner; runner up in maiden hurdles at Kilbeggan (2m, good), Killarney (20.5f, yld-sft) and Sligo (20.5f, good) last summer; poor when last seen at Listowel in September and probably best watched after break in first handicap unless money comes.
runners.comment: Runner up in three maidens last summer, off since September, watch unless market speaks
runners.owner: Treaty Retail Invest' Holdings Ltd/Denis Murphy B'stock Ltd

runners.name: Chemdawg
runners.spotlight: In good form; runner up here over 21.5f (soft); breakthrough win on latest at Kilbeggan (2m3f, gd-yld), making all to score decisively by 8l; raised 8lb but should make another bold bid; big player.
runners.comment: Made all for decisive breakthrough win at Kilbeggan, up 8lb but big player again

runners.name: Committee Meeting
runners.spotlight: Point winner; displayed promise in maiden hurdles at Punchestown and Thurles; poor form last year in three chase starts; heavy defeat after break on handicap debut at Cork in April; can only be watched in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat after break on h'cap debut at Cork, blinkers go on, best watched for now

runners.name: Swelltime
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 4-33 over hurdles; last won at Killarney off 4lb lower last July (20.5f, yld-sft); good ground is fine but lacks a run and overall record when fresh suggests he'll come on for this run.
runners.comment: 4-33 over hurdles, won off 4lb lower at Killarney last July, lacks a run, might need this

runners.name: Work Place
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens and similar story in first handicap at Thurles (2m6f, good) when tailed off at 80-1 in March 2025; eased 4lb but off since; best watched.
runners.comment: Tailed off on handicap debut In March 2025, down 4lb but off since, hard to fancy for now

runners.name: Another Glendaars
runners.spotlight: Last sighted at Fairyhouse in December 2024 when beaten 16l on handicap debut; huge layoff to overcome but is worth a market check on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Well beaten on h'cap bow in Dec' 2024, off since, new yard, check the market

runners.name: King Of The Greys
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; very modest efforts in maiden hurdles; doesn't look obviously well treated; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant, ordinary maiden form, not obviously well treated, watch

runners.name: Takarengo
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; record of 1-31 over hurdles; couple of promising placed efforts after joining this yard last year but modest form so far in 2026 on the Flat and over hurdles; beaten 23l off 2lb higher at Clonmel latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: 1-31 over hurdles, beaten 23l off 2lb higher at Clonmel latest, others preferred

races.race_name: Frankie Dunworth Memorial Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Moonshine Girl
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden was a decent third over fences at Sligo in October; not a bad effort when two places behind Navy Waves on return at Cork; entitled to come on for that effort.
runners.comment: 17-race maiden; not a bad effort when 2 spots behind Navy Waves on return at Cork

runners.name: Western Model
runners.spotlight: Punchestown hurdles winner in May 2024 off 90; record of 0-9 chasing for Paul Nolan in recent times but hit the frame on several occasions; off since November; first run for new yard; market can guide.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles in 2024; 0-9 chasing for Paul Nolan; new yard after a break; watch market

runners.name: Spellacy's Cross
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back handicap chase winner at Clonmel in October; unable to take advantage of lower hurdles mark in four recent runs but fair third of 18 at Punchestown (3m, good) on latest; big player again despite inability to win over timber to date a concern.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner can race off much lower hurdles mark and should be bang there
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F O'Neill

runners.name: Laska Du Breuil
runners.spotlight: Modest form overall; ninth of 18 at Punchestown in December (2m1f, yielding) his best effort; pulled up before two out at Kilbeggan last month; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Pulled up before two out at Kilbeggan last month; new headgear combination

runners.name: Thats All
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 21 starts over hurdles but was generally reliable last summer/autumn; 2m an inadequate test on return at Punchestown this month; better expected with the cobwebs blown away.
runners.comment: 2m an inadequate test on return at Punchestown this month; better expected now

runners.name: Sineads Fancy
runners.spotlight: Sole win came at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) nearly two years ago; third at Thurles over same trip last October her best run since; midfield at Clonmel on return.
runners.comment: Sole win came at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) nearly two years ago; midfield on return

runners.name: Cozone
runners.spotlight: Bounced back with a bang when easily winning at Downpatrick (2m6f, yielding to soft) off career-low mark on return in March; failed to back that up from 10lb higher at Down Royal but remains fairly treated on pick of old form.
runners.comment: Easy winner on return at Downpatrick; didn't back it up next time from 10lb higher

runners.name: Barryroe Madam
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-22 over hurdles, with the win coming in Listowel handicap in May last year (23.5f, good) off 85; lost form in final start for Brian McMahon and two poor efforts for this yard since; best watched.
runners.comment: Won Listowel handicap in May last year (23.5f, good) off 85 but out of form a while now

runners.name: Embrace The Day
runners.spotlight: Promise when winning a point for Jonathan Fogarty; didn't show much in first five hurdle starts but best effort yet at Kilbeggan (19.5f, good to yielding) in first-time tongue-tie (retained) when fourth of 15; in the mix up in trip.
runners.comment: Best effort yet at Kilbeggan in first-time tongue-tie (retained) when fourth; in the mix

runners.name: The Sugarhill Lady
runners.spotlight: Largely struggled in handicaps until much more encouraging effort off reduced mark at Cork last month, staying on into fourth over 2m (yielding); similar effort when fourth at Clonmel a fortnight ago (19.5f, good to yielding); respected dropped 2lb for local yard.
runners.comment: Two respectable runs of late; respected dropped 2lb for local yard
runners.owner: Eamon Leahy & Seamus O'Riordan & Carmel Walsh

runners.name: Barrowline
runners.spotlight: First sign of ability when hinting this sort of trip would suit when staying on for 28l fifth of 16 at Tramore last month; pulled up after mistake three out at Sligo (3m2f) on latest; this track could suit better.
runners.comment: Pulled up after mistake three out at Sligo (3m2f) on latest; this track could suit better

runners.name: Lady Aquarelle
runners.spotlight: Fell in two of three points; three maiden hurdle runs offered little; only minor promise when midfield on handicap debut at Clonmel (19.5f) a fortnight ago; needs to find improvement.
runners.comment: Only minor promise when midfield on handicap debut at Clonmel (19.5f) a fortnight ago

runners.name: Jack Inthe Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Modest form in point-to-points and maiden hurdles; pulled up after never counting on last month's handicap debut at Down Royal; headgear combination needs to bring serious improvement; engaged 7.49 Wexford on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Headgear combination needs to bring serious improvement; engaged 7.49 Wexford on Wednesday

runners.name: John Dixen
runners.spotlight: Never a factor in four strong maiden hurdles at 2m-2m1f; could prove to be a different proposition now handicapping; cheekpieces; 3lb wrong; betting will provide clues.
runners.comment: Could prove to be a different proposition now handicapping; cheekpieces; 3lb wrong

races.race_name: Frankie Dunworth Memorial Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Head For Home
runners.spotlight: Standout effort a 4.75l second of 20 at Punchestown last October off 80 (2m4f, good to yielding); pulled up on return at Down Royal but much-improved fourth of 21 at Cork (24.5f, good) this month when making good headway from rear; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Improved fourth of 21 at Cork (24.5f, good) this month when making good headway from rear

runners.name: Anysignofhim
runners.spotlight: Maiden from 11 starts over hurdles; set a stiff task in claiming hurdle at Clonmel last time; this a more realistic opportunity; enjoys good ground.
runners.comment: Stiff task in claiming hurdle at Clonmel last time; this a more realistic opportunity

runners.name: Mickey Hulie
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner is yet to score from nine runs under rules; claims on the basis of his neck runner-up finish at Naas (3m, soft to heavy); below that level when favourite here eight days later; disappointed in cheekpieces (left off) at Kilbeggan; solid chance if he handles the ground.
runners.comment: Dual point winner is yet to score from nine runs under rules; claims on his Naas run

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 42 starts but solid effort over hurdles at Sligo (2m4f, good) on penultimate when staying on dourly to take second (Island McCoo back in fourth); ran no race here over fences last week; hard to trust.
runners.comment: Good claims on her Sligo second but she's still a maiden after 42 starts; hard to trust

runners.name: Queen Kulina
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran some good races in defeat after a lengthy absence last season, the standout when beaten 1.5l into fourth at Galway (3m, yielding) off 1lb higher in October; was still travelling okay when a faller four out on 3m Punchestown return 15 days ago.
runners.comment: Good Galway effort; still travelling okay when a faller four out on 3m Punchestown return

runners.name: The Striker Dylan
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to chasing; survived a mistake four out on his way to winning at Sligo in October (2m5f, good to yielding); not as good next time when fifth from 5lb higher at Thurles; needed the run on return last year.
runners.comment: Improved for the switch to chasing last season; needed the run on return last year

runners.name: Doing It In Style
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in bumpers/maidens and well beaten in two handicaps; failed to improve for better ground at Downpatrick on latest; cheekpieces are now tried.
runners.comment: Failed to improve for better ground at Downpatrick on latest; cheekpieces are now tried

runners.name: Charlie's Gift
runners.spotlight: Some promise in maidens; fair third on handicap debut at Kilbeggan (18.5f) off 100 last summer but failed to build on that; midfield at Clonmel on return; others preferred.
runners.comment: Fair third on handicap debut at Kilbeggan off 100 last summer but failed to build on that

runners.name: Something Simple
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in opening two handicap runs and well beaten last time at Clonmel; others preferred.
runners.comment: Pulled up in opening 2 handicap runs and well beaten lto at Clonmel; others preferred
runners.owner: Something Simple Syndicate/J C Hurley Et Al

runners.name: Rizedda
runners.spotlight: Best effort in maidens came at Tramore when she finished 8l fourth (2m5f, good) last August; mistake three out costly when well beaten at Listowel on handicap debut; will enjoy the ground; player on return from 243-day absence.
runners.comment: Tramore run was encouraging; mistake three out at Listowel; will enjoy this ground

runners.name: Craic Eile
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins came at Tramore but nothing since 2021; out of form last summer; modest fifth at Wexford on return.
runners.comment: Both hurdle wins came at Tramore but nothing since 2021; modest fifth at Wexford on return

runners.name: Je Suis
runners.spotlight: Yet to make an impact in ten hurdle/chase starts although best hurdles effort was at this course in April last year; disappointed when sent off the well-backed favourite for Killarney handicap chase in October; 235 days off.
runners.comment: Disappointed when sent off the well-backed fav' for Killarney handicap chase in October

runners.name: Island McCoo
runners.spotlight: Chase winner is rated 4lb lower in this sphere; best handicap hurdle effort yet on latest at Sligo (2m4f, good) when never-nearer fourth of 16 (Love Like This second); the step up in trip on a more galloping track should suit.
runners.comment: Stayed on well at Sligo; the step up in trip and this track may suit his running style

runners.name: Poetic Focus
runners.spotlight: Promise in two of her six runs last season, staying on into fifth over 20.5f (good) at Bellewstown off 95 and again over 22.5f (good to yielding) when fourth at Galway; fell at Clonmel; testing ground an excuse at Gowran; very interesting on return/stable debut for Andy Slattery.
runners.comment: Promise in two of her six runs last season on decent ground; interesting on yard debut

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares INH Flat Race

runners.name: Streamhill
runners.spotlight: Modest form in both points; definite promise both starts under Rules when beaten around 5l in bumpers at Cork (2m, yielding) and Kilbeggan (16.5f, gd-yld); the form of her Cork run has worked out and she has leading claims on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Solid efforts in bumpers at Cork and Kilbeggan, leading claims on debut for new yard

runners.name: Brave Lady
runners.spotlight: Sent off at just 9-1 on debut at Galway last year and ran a fine race to finish a 3.5l third and the winner has since placed in Listed company; out of depth on first run since in Grade 2 bumper at Aintree in April; could do better returned to calmer waters.
runners.comment: Promise on debut at Galway last year, out of depth latest, could do better now

runners.name: Mistakenforanother
runners.spotlight: Offered something to build on when fourth on debut at Clonmel but was still beaten 18.5l; should improve for that initial experience and place claims if she does.
runners.comment: Promise on debut at Clonmel, place claims if improves for that initial experience

runners.name: Online Order
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George mare; dam point winner, sister to winners Shantou Rock (smart 2m hurdle/chase) and Ever So Cool (2m hurdle); hooded for debut and probably best watched.
runners.comment: Order Of St George mare, dam point winner, hooded for debut, probably best watched

runners.name: Getaway Dreams
runners.spotlight: Getaway mare; half-sister to 2m chase winner Goaheadwiththeplan; dam unraced sister to 2m7f hurdler winner Oscar Maguire, out of sister to 1m6f Flat and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Dante's Battle; Aodha's Arrow probably yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Getaway mare, half-sister to 2m chase winner, Aodha's Arrow looks yard's main hope

runners.name: Land Me Safe
runners.spotlight: Footstepsinthesand mare; sister to bumper winner Walk On The Beach, half-sister to point winner The Gooner; dam unraced sister to useful 2m4f hurdle/chase winner Go All The Way, half-sister to bumper winner; likely type on paper from top yard.
runners.comment: Footstepsinthesand mare, sister to bumper winner, likely type on paper, top yard

runners.name: Lexenna
runners.spotlight: Gamut mare; dam 1m3f/1m4f winner (RPR 72), half-sister to German 1m1f/1m2f winner Flick Flack; likely best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Gamut mare, dam modest German winner, watch on debut unless market speaks

runners.name: Saoirses Choice
runners.spotlight: Elusive Pimpernel mare; dam 6f AW winner (RPR 59), half-sister to 1m4f Flat and useful 2m hurdle winner Palace Rock, out of 1m1f/1m2f Flat and 2m hurdle winner; yard 0-5 in bumpers last five seasons; watch.
runners.comment: Elusive Pimpernel mare; dam 6f AW winner, yard 0-5 in bumpers last five seasons

runners.name: Withmylittleeye
runners.spotlight: Soldier Of Fortune mare; dam 1m AW 2yo winner (stayed 1m4f), sister to 5f winner Misty Sparkler, half-sister to 1m Listed winner St Moritz, out of US 6f-1m winner; speed on dam side of pedigree; best watched.
runners.comment: Solider Of Fortune mare, dam 1m 2yo winner, best watched on debut

runners.name: Wild Eagle
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and completely tailed off on debut in a 2m2f Sligo bumper 16 days ago; impossible to fancy after that effort.
runners.comment: 150-1 and tailed off on debut at Sligo, impossible to fancy after that

runners.name: Aodha's Arrow
runners.spotlight: Form of debut effort at Cork (16.5f, good) has received a couple of boosts but she didn't build on it when beaten 14.5l back there on latest (2m1f, good); capable of better and too soon to write off.
runners.comment: Solid effort on debut at Cork, not as good there latest but capable of better

races.race_name: Summer Plate Ladies Day Book Now Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cockney Cleopatra
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when fifth of nine in Clonmel maiden (2m, soft) in November but pulled up twice for new stable at Fontwell this spring (2m3f/2m1f); could be considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Pulled up twice for new stable this spring; considered only if lively in the betting

runners.name: Wahraan
runners.spotlight: Has some useful Flat form and kept on steadily from a long way off the pace to take third on hurdle debut at Wetherby (2m, soft) in November; subsequent Grade 2 defeat is easily overlooked but he has become a very awkward ride, often refusing to settle and/or hanging; comes with risk.
runners.comment: Has the raw ability but is an awkward ride nowadays and comes with risk

runners.name: Jessie Jump Jet
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but went close in Class 5 handicap at Warwick (2m5f, good) this month and should be fine over today's shorter trip; might have been found a good opportunity.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles but went close in recent Warwick handicap; high on the list

runners.name: Frisky Whiskey
runners.spotlight: Showed only a glimmer of promise before weakening into well-held sixth on recent bumper debut at Southwell (good); needs to take a good step forward on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Showed only a glimmer of promise before weakening into sixth on recent bumper debut

runners.name: Bailey's Luck
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when 200-1 for 1m AW maiden on Flat debut in September; now switched to hurdling with a hood fitted.
runners.comment: Well beaten when 200-1 for 1m AW maiden on Flat debut in September; hurdle debut today

runners.name: Popalot
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in two course bumpers last season but made low-key start to hurdling career when well beaten at 40-1 here (2m) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Low-key start to hurdling career when well-beaten 40-1 shot here (2m) three weeks ago

runners.name: Aimeric
runners.spotlight: Smart on the Flat at his best but signed off for Karl Burke with two disappointing runs in the autumn and was rather hesitant at some of the flights when disappointing favourite on last month's stable/hurdle debut; subsequently tailed off in major Flat handicap; may yet get his act together over hurdles but he is very tricky to weigh up at the moment.
runners.comment: Smart on the Flat at his best but disappointing on hurdle debut; tricky to weigh up now

runners.name: Prince Abu
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since his two very underwhelming hurdle runs for Derek Shaw in summer 2024 and returned from 318-day absence with 19l defeat off basement Flat mark last month; swaps hood for refitted cheekpieces; tongue-tie a first.
runners.comment: Both hurdle runs in 2024 were underwhelming; well beaten off basement Flat mark last month

races.race_name: Geoff Handley's Grand 80th Handicap Chase

runners.name: El Muchacho
runners.spotlight: Won twice on good ground (2m5f/2m3f) for this new stable last season and has been placed on last three appearances, most recently when second over C&D (good) three weeks ago; 1lb lower today and seems very likely to be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Won twice last season and posted another good effort when second over C&D this month
runners.owner: T J Stubbins and M E Sowersby

runners.name: Play By The Rules
runners.spotlight: Returned from long absence with a promising stable debut at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March, when he travelled strongly for a long way, and he was unlucky to be brought down fairly early on at Hexham this month; remains well handicapped on old form; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Made a promising stable debut in March; unlucky to be brought down last time

runners.name: Get A Superstar
runners.spotlight: Placed on chasing debut in December 2024 but raced only once last season, when modest seventh in Sedgefield race won by El Muchacho in November; back from another layoff today; current ability is hard to gauge.
runners.comment: Modest seventh on sole appearance last season; returns from another break today

runners.name: Livano Bello
runners.spotlight: Didn't fire over hurdles at Perth a fortnight ago and is now 0-13 in that sphere but he's generally pretty consistent and will be suited by the trip-and-ground combination on this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Didn't fire on latest hurdling start but is largely consistent; chase debut today

races.race_name: Family Extravaganza Raceday On Sunday 5th July Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Cher Tara
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when fifth of eight at Kelso (2m5f, good) three weeks ago and continues to slip down the weights but has modest 2-21 strike-rate over hurdles; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly at Kelso this month but has modest strike-rate; others appeal more

runners.name: Parish Star
runners.spotlight: 2-5 for new stable this year; gained the wins on heavy and good to soft ground but coped perfectly well with good ground when close third at Kelso (2m5f; first-time tongue-tie) last month; hood (worn for three of her last four races) is discarded; likely contender again.
runners.comment: 2-5 for new stable this year; good third at Kelso last month; likely contender again

runners.name: Time Was
runners.spotlight: 0-19 under rules but returned from a break with a clear second over C&D (good to soft) in November and made the frame on all three subsequent appearances; returns from another break today; can make her presence felt.
runners.comment: 0-19 under rules but in the frame all four starts since clear C&D second in November

runners.name: Mistral Blue
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m3f hurdle winner on good ground; pulled up on soft at end of last year but returned from a break with a quite encouraging fifth of 12 at Warwick (2m5f, good) this month and enters calculations here.
runners.comment: Returned from break with a quite encouraging run this month; enters calculations

runners.name: Lone Star
runners.spotlight: Capitalised upon some considerable help from the handicapper when winning over C&D (good) just under a year ago and returns here after a decent effort at Kelso this month, but she's nothing like the force of old and has become inconsistent.
runners.comment: C&D winner last spring but nothing like the force of old and has become inconsistent

runners.name: Little Soiree
runners.spotlight: Now 20lb lower than when winning over 2m for Chris Gordon just over a year ago but has badly lost her way; huge leap of faith is required to support her.
runners.comment: Has badly lost her way since winning for another stable last spring

runners.name: Royal Maggs
runners.spotlight: Last of four when upped to 2m7f for last month's handicap debut and only eighth of ten (behind Lone Star) when dropped back to 2m5f at Kelso three weeks ago; not beaten far in either of those races but needs to up her game; usual hood is left off.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in her first two handicaps (2m7f/2m5f) but needs improvement
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing, S Knowles & Partners

runners.name: Lady Fortune
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six hurdle runs, most recently when pulled up in soft-ground handicap on stable debut in January; combination of wind surgery, first-time hood and return to good ground could plausibly prompt a better performance here, though; worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Pulled up on stable debut but the ground was soft; had wind op since; worth market check

races.race_name: Anji Fletcher Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sun Art
runners.spotlight: Yet to make any further progress since winning a 2m Ludlow novice in March 2025 but posted a sound effort when second off today's mark at Hereford (2m, good) last month and is work another crack at this sort of trip.
runners.comment: Second off this mark at Hereford (2m) last month and worth another crack at this trip

runners.name: Lord Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: Displayed considerable improvement when winning easily by 12l on last month's handicap hurdle debut at Hereford (2m, good); managed only third under 7lb penalty here (2m2f, good) eight days later and is another 5lb higher today, but he moves up in trip again and remains of interest.
runners.comment: Won easily on last month's handicap debut (2m); held under penalty since; still considered

runners.name: Snatch A Glance
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to win 2m Ludlow maiden in the autumn and was a very respectable third of six in novice event over that C&D (again on good) last month; moves up in trip for handicap debut; should have more to offer.
runners.comment: 2m Ludlow novice winner; up in trip for handicap debut; should have more to offer

runners.name: Jackstell
runners.spotlight: Won three consecutive small-field races in the autumn, the latest a Plumpton handicap (2m4f, good); absent since struggling on soft ground there in December but remains lightly raced and could plausibly still have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Won three small-field races in the autumn; off since struggling on soft ground in December

runners.name: Crebilly
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler/chaser in his prime; well below best during a fairly light campaign last season (four hurdle runs; cheekpieces added last time) but will appreciate this ease in grade and has slipped to a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Well below best during light campaign last season but has slipped to a dangerous mark

runners.name: High Dancer
runners.spotlight: Didn't see out 3m when well-held third at Doncaster (good to soft) in March but ran well for a long way and is now back on same mark as for his clear second behind a useful rival there (2m3f, soft) in November; also suited by good ground; could be a tough nut to crack if aided by new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Didn't see out 3m last time but still ran well; big player if aided by new cheekpieces

runners.name: Nitty Gritty
runners.spotlight: 0-7 for current stable (since the autumn) and has a very mixed record but ran well when second in refitted cheekpieces at Kelso (2m5f, good) this month and was unlucky to come to grief at Uttoxeter recently; remains on a good mark; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Has a mixed record for this stable (since the autumn) but ran well when second this month

races.race_name: Dine In The Altior's Resturant Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Catchim
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was left clear just after final fence to score over C&D (good) last month, opening his chase account at the 12th attempt; evidently in good nick and subsequent 4lb rise is not excessive.
runners.comment: Left clear after final fence to score over C&D last month; 4lb rise not excessive

runners.name: Cawthorne Cracker
runners.spotlight: Not at his best at Stratford 11 days ago and now 0-6 over fences but he had returned from a break with a pretty good third over C&D (good) last month and his current mark is workable.
runners.comment: Below form at Stratford recently but was placed over C&D last month; a possible

runners.name: Intenzo
runners.spotlight: Made a promising chase/handicap debut when clear second at Sedgefield (2m, good; form well advertised by winner since) in March but three lesser efforts have followed and his tendency to race too freely remains a worry.
runners.comment: Good second on chase/handicap debut in March but three lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Lheur De Gloire
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but shaped well before fading into fourth of 13 at Southwell (2m, good; first run after wind op) this month and Ben Pauling is evidently keen to launch his chasing career; interesting.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but shaped well on latest outing; interesting on chase debut

runners.name: Walk The Plank
runners.spotlight: Battled well for narrow success at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March, his third start over fences; that form looks modest and he's 4lb higher here but further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Form of his hard-fought Huntingdon win looks modest but further progress is possible

races.race_name: Half Year Annual Badges On Sale Now Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bunker Bay
runners.spotlight: Reportedly had breathing issue when well beaten at Perth last month and is now 0-9 over hurdles but his clear second to next-time-out winner Ruler Legend over C&D (good) two starts ago was a good effort; big player if back at that level.
runners.comment: Clear second to Ruler Legend over C&D last month; had a breathing issue on latest outing

runners.name: Ruler Legend
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in excellent form, winning twice here (2m/2m2f) on good ground, and is probably still on a workable mark; big chance.
runners.comment: Ended last season in excellent form, winning twice here (2m/2m2f); big chance

runners.name: Made All
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer; yet to make a significant impact over hurdles but was given a lot to do before finishing well for third over C&D (good) on handicap debut three weeks ago, and his recent Flat run was perfectly respectable.
runners.comment: Given a lot to do before finishing well for third over C&D on handicap hurdle debut

runners.name: Paragon
runners.spotlight: 0-11 on the Flat and 0-4 over hurdles; made a fairly encouraging handicap hurdle debut when sixth of 13 at Southwell (2m, good) this month, though, and might be boosted by refitted cheekpieces here.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging handicap hurdle debut this month; refitted cheekpieces might help

runners.name: Red Trail
runners.spotlight: Belied 80-1 odds when placed off today's mark in this race last year but was well beaten on next hurdling start and has been absent since some disappointing Flat runs in the autumn; too risky.
runners.comment: Placed at big odds in this race last year but overall profile is very unconvincing

runners.name: Assorda
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare; fortunate to win small-field Catterick novice (1m7f, good) for Paula Smith on British debut in March 2025 and soundly beaten in all five subsequent handicaps, the last four times for new stable this year; drops back in trip (from 2m4f).
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on all four starts for new stable this year (2m1f-2m4f)

runners.name: Melosa
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on the Flat but was pulled up when 100-1 for handicap hurdle debut here (2m4f, good) in April; drops back in trip today; can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 100-1 for last month's handicap hurdle debut; drops back from 2m4f
